That the EnMS conforms to planned arrangements for energy management
What organizational role must ensure the internal audit results are utilized in the review process?
The management representative(s) reports to top management on the performance of the EnMS (which includes audit results)
What must the internal audit confirm about the organization's documented targets (3.17)?
That the EnMS conforms with the energy objectives and targets established
How does the guidance distinguish between an energy audit/assessment and an internal audit of the Energy Management System?

An energy audit or assessment involves a detailed review of performance, typically based on measurement and observation, leading to recommendations for improvement . This is not the same concept as an internal audit of the EnMS or an internal audit of the energy performance of the EnMS, which is a systematic check of conformity against the standard’s requirements
If an organization chooses to combine its EnMS internal audits with other internal audits, what clarification is required?
If an organization decides to combine the audit of its energy management system with other internal audits, the intent and scope of each audit should be clearly defined
What must be done with the records of the internal audit results?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained and reported to top management
What types of documents or information can serve as examples of "other requirements" to which an organization might subscribe?
Examples of "other requirements" can include agreements with customers, voluntary principles or codes of practice, and participation in voluntary programs
What are examples of personnel considered to be "working on behalf of the organization" in the context of the energy review?
Examples of personnel working on behalf of the organization include service contractors, part-time personnel, and temporary staff
What is the concept of a multi-variable model, and where is it used in the EnMS?

A multi-variable model is a type of Energy Performance Indicator (EnPI) . EnPIs can be expressed as a simple metric, ratio, or a more complex model, and the organization can choose those that best inform the energy performance of their operation
When procuring energy-using products, why is it necessary to assess performance over the expected operating lifetime?

It is necessary to assess energy use, consumption, and efficiency over the planned or expected operating lifetime when procuring energy-using products and equipment that are expected to have a significant impact on the organization's energy performance . This ensures that the procurement process acts as an opportunity to improve energy performance through the use of more efficient products and services
What is the role of technical documentation in the control of documents?

Technical documentation must be controlled where appropriate, as part of the overall requirement that documents needed by the International Standard and the EnMS must be controlled . This control includes ensuring documents are legible, readily identifiable, and that external documents are controlled
How does the organization ensure that obsolete documents are managed correctly?

The organization must establish, implement, and maintain procedures to prevent the unintended use of obsolete documents . Furthermore, any obsolete documents that must be retained for legal or knowledge preservation purposes must be suitably identified
What should an organization consider when planning its response to contingency or emergency situations regarding energy?

When planning for potential disasters, emergency situations, or contingencies, which includes procuring equipment, an organization may choose to include energy performance in determining how it will react to these situations
How does top management fulfill the reporting requirement concerning results measurement?

Top management is responsible for ensuring that results are measured and reported at determined intervals . This involves reviewing and reporting on both the performance of the EnMS and the overall energy performance
What key elements must an organization establish for the effective control of documents?

The organization must establish procedures to approve documents for adequacy prior to issue, periodically review and update them, ensure changes and current revision status are identified, and ensure that relevant versions are available at points of use
What must the organization consider regarding the complexity of its system when defining its energy measurement plan?

The organization must define and implement an energy measurement plan that is appropriate to the size and complexity of the organization and its monitoring and measurement equipment . The necessary measurement means and methods are determined by the organization itself
What must the organization ensure regarding its energy management activities to facilitate effective energy management?
The management representative is responsible for defining and communicating responsibilities and authorities throughout the organization in order to facilitate effective energy management
How should an organization evaluate operations associated with significant energy use to meet policy requirements?

An organization should evaluate those operations associated with its identified significant energy use and ensure they are conducted in a manner that will control or reduce the adverse impacts associated with them . This is essential for fulfilling the requirements of its energy policy and meeting its objectives and targets
What is the role of an appropriate level of management in supporting the management representative?

Top management must appoint a management representative, who in turn must identify person(s) authorized by an appropriate level of management to work with them in support of energy management activities
How must the management representative promote organizational commitment to energy goals?
The management representative has the responsibility and authority to promote awareness of the energy policy and objectives at all levels of the organization
What is included in the outputs of an energy audit typically?

The outputs of an energy audit typically include information on current consumption and performance . They can also be accompanied by a series of ranked recommendations for improvement in terms of energy performance
What must the organization do to ensure that the necessary data is available for the energy review analysis?

The organization must develop, record, and maintain an energy review by analyzing energy use and consumption based on measurement and other data . This analysis must detail the evaluation of past and present energy use and consumption
When is it necessary for the organization to review its Energy Performance Indicators (EnPIs) against the energy baseline?
EnPIs shall be reviewed and compared to the energy baseline as appropriate . This comparison helps ensure that the indicators accurately reflect and measure performance against the reference point
What is included in the concept of "updating the energy review"?

Updating the energy review means updating the information specifically related to the analysis of energy use, the determination of significance of energy use areas, and the determination of opportunities for improving energy performance
What is the implication for two different organizations operating similar processes but having different energy performances, regarding conformity?

This International Standard does not establish absolute requirements for energy performance . Consequently, two organizations carrying out similar operations, but having different energy performance, can both conform to its requirements
How do the inputs to the management review ensure follow-up and accountability?

The inputs explicitly require the inclusion of follow-up actions from previous management reviews . This ensures that prior decisions and incomplete actions are reviewed for status and accountability in the current review cycle
What is the organization required to consider when designing its measurement methodology?

The organization must define and implement an energy measurement plan and must also define and periodically review its measurement needs . It is the responsibility of the organization to determine the means and methods of measurement, which may range from simple utility meters to complex monitoring systems
How must the operational controls be communicated to personnel?
The appropriate communication of the operational controls must be made to personnel working for, or on behalf of, the organization
What specific requirements must the energy policy meet regarding the organization's energy use?
The energy policy must be appropriate to the nature and scale of the organization’s energy use and consumption
In what media can the Energy Management System documentation be maintained?
The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain the necessary information in paper, electronic, or any other medium
What specific role do records play in documenting conformity?
Records are necessary to demonstrate conformity to the requirements of the organization's EnMS and the International Standard
What happens if the EnPIs no longer reflect organizational energy use and consumption?
If the EnPIs no longer reflect the organizational energy use and consumption, this is one of the conditions that requires adjustments to be made to the energy baseline
Why are the requirements for monitoring, measurement, and analysis critical to the system?

They are critical because they ensure that the key characteristics of the operations that determine energy performance are monitored, measured, and analyzed . This allows for the evaluation of actual versus expected consumption and the effectiveness of action plans
What must the organization consider regarding procurement as an opportunity?

Procurement is considered an opportunity to improve energy performance through the use of more efficient products and services . It is also seen as an opportunity to work with the supply chain and influence its energy behavior
What must the organization do to ensure that external documents necessary for the planning and operation of the EnMS are correctly managed?
The organization must ensure that documents of external origin determined to be necessary for the planning and operation of the EnMS are identified and their distribution controlled
What must be recorded concerning the results of the design activity?

The results of the design activity shall be recorded . This involves incorporating the results of the energy performance evaluation into the relevant project specifications, design, and procurement activities
How is flexibility granted to the organization regarding the review of the management system elements?

The management review should cover the entire scope of the energy management system . However, it is not required that all elements of the energy management system be reviewed at once, and the review process may take place over a period of time
How does the energy management team achieve its objective of delivering improved energy performance?

The energy management team, which can be cross-functional in larger organizations, ensures the delivery of energy performance improvements by supporting the effective implementation of the EnMS activities
What is required to ensure objectivity and impartiality in the internal audit process?
The selection of auditors and the conduct of audits shall ensure the objectivity and impartiality of the audit process
When is an organization required to establish criteria for operational and maintenance activities?

Criteria for the effective operation and maintenance of significant energy uses must be established and set in situations where their absence could lead to a significant deviation from effective energy performance
What must the organization ensure regarding its control over external documents?
The organization must ensure that external documents that are deemed necessary for the planning and operation of the EnMS are identified and their distribution is controlled
In the context of energy management, what does "normalizing" the energy baseline involve?

Normalizing an energy baseline involves using variables which affect energy use and/or consumption to adjust the baseline . Examples of such variables include production level or degree days (outdoor temperature)
What action is required when updating documents in the Energy Management System?
Procedures must ensure that documents are periodically reviewed and updated as necessary, and that changes and the current revision status of documents are identified
How does the EnMS help an organization demonstrate conformity?

An EnMS enables an organization to achieve its policy commitments, take necessary action to improve its energy performance, and ultimately demonstrate the conformity of the system to the requirements of this International Standard
What must the organization communicate about the evaluation criteria when procuring products?
When procuring products, the organization must inform suppliers that the procurement is partially evaluated on the basis of energy performance
What is the goal of incorporating energy performance evaluation results into project specifications?

The goal is to ensure that the results of the energy performance evaluation are appropriately incorporated into the specification, design, and procurement activities of new, modified, or renovated projects
What is the relationship between the energy policy and the energy performance improvement actions?

The energy policy is the driver for energy performance improvement . The planning process ensures that energy planning leads to activities that continually improve energy performance, consistent with the policy
What must be done to ensure that all relevant versions of applicable documents are accessible?
Procedures must ensure that relevant versions of applicable documents are available at points of use
What information must be included in the EnMS documentation regarding the policy's framework?
The EnMS documentation must include the energy policy, which provides the framework for setting and reviewing energy objectives and targets
What happens to the energy performance assessment results during the design phase?
The energy performance evaluation results must be incorporated, where appropriate, into the specification, design, and procurement activities of the relevant project(s)
When is the management review required to occur?
The management review is required to occur at planned intervals
What must be recorded concerning the methodology for EnPIs?
The methodology for determining and updating the EnPIs shall be recorded and regularly reviewed
Who is responsible for providing sufficient resources for the EnMS?
Top management is responsible for providing the necessary resources, including financial and technical resources, needed to establish, implement, maintain, and improve the EnMS
What concept is illustrated by the provided Figure A.one?

Figure A.one provides an illustrative conceptual representation of energy performance, showing its relationship with energy use, energy consumption, energy efficiency, energy intensity, and other relevant factors
What must be the focus of the organization’s review of its activities during the energy planning stage?
Energy planning shall involve a review of the organization's activities that can affect energy performance
What must the organization ensure about its energy objectives and targets concerning the energy review?

When establishing and reviewing objectives and targets, the organization must take into account the significant energy uses and opportunities to improve energy performance identified in the energy review
What must be done with the records of calibration and other means used to establish accuracy?
Records of calibration and other means of establishing the accuracy and repeatability of monitoring and measurement equipment shall be maintained
What must the organization ensure regarding personnel’s understanding of their roles?
The organization must ensure that personnel are aware of their roles, responsibilities, and authorities in achieving the requirements of the Energy Management System
When must the organization make decisions about external communication?
The organization must decide whether to communicate externally about its energy policy, EnMS, and energy performance, and this decision must be documented
How can top management support the importance of energy management through employee involvement?
Top management can support the importance of energy management through employee involvement activities such as empowerment, motivation, recognition, training, and rewards, and participation
What is the commitment related to continuous energy performance improvement contained within the energy policy?
The energy policy must include a specific commitment to continual improvement in energy performance
What is the primary requirement regarding the frequency of conducting internal audits of the EnMS?
The organization shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals
What three characteristics define the internal audit process itself, according to its definition?
Internal audit is a systematic, independent and documented process
What is the general purpose of evaluating evidence during an internal audit?
To determine the extent to which requirements are fulfilled
What must be developed before internal audits can commence?
An audit plan and schedule shall be developed
What planning input, derived from previous control activities, must be considered when setting the audit schedule?
The results of previous audits
What mandatory action must be taken with the documentation produced by the internal audit?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained
To whom must the records of the audit results be reported?
The audit results shall be reported to top management
What term describes the process of assessing energy performance based on measurement and observation, which is not the same as an internal audit of the EnMS?
An energy audit or assessment
What specific purpose does the EnMS internal audit serve regarding continual improvement?
It ensures that the EnMS improves energy performance
What must the audit plan and schedule take into consideration regarding past audit findings?
The results of previous audits
What management review input specifically relies on the internal audit process?
EnMS audit results [68 f)]
How is the internal audit process characterized in terms of documentation?
It is a documented process
What does the internal audit confirm about the implementation status of the EnMS?
That it is effectively implemented and maintained
What must the organization ensure regarding the documentation of the audit process?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained
What must be true about the audit process to ensure its credibility?
It must ensure objectivity and impartiality
What must be developed taking into consideration the status of audited processes?
An audit plan and schedule shall be developed
What is the relationship between the internal audit and the organization's planned arrangements for energy management?
The audit ensures conformity to these planned arrangements
What specific management function receives the audit results report?
Top management
What must the internal audit process be capable of obtaining?
Evidence
What must the organization ensure about the competence of the personnel conducting the internal audit?
The persons conducting the audit should be competent
What must the organization consider about the processes and areas when prioritizing the audit schedule?
Their status and importance
In the context of the PDCA cycle, what phase does the Internal Audit fall under?
The Check phase
What specific energy performance result must the internal audit verify is occurring within the EnMS?
That the EnMS improves energy performance
What documentation requirement is linked directly to the internal audit results?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained
What must the organization establish to ensure audits are performed in a structured manner?
An audit plan and schedule
What two qualities of the audit process are ensured by the selection of auditors?
Objectivity and impartiality
If an organization uses external persons to conduct the audit, on whose behalf are they working?
They are working on its behalf
What type of evidence is the internal audit process designed to obtain?
Objective evidence
What is the required state of the EnMS that the internal audit must confirm, besides implementation?
That it is effectively maintained
If an organization fails to audit at planned intervals, which requirement is violated?
The requirement to conduct internal audits at planned intervals
What kind of process is the internal audit, in terms of independence?
It is an independent process
What specific commitment (3.2) is verified through the audit's check on energy performance improvement?
The commitment to continual improvement
What must be maintained for all audit findings?
Records of the audit results
If the internal audit highlights a weakness in a process, what management activity (4.6.4) is this output directed toward?
Reviewing nonconformities or potential nonconformities [65 a)]
What is the general term for the requirements that the internal audit seeks to determine the extent of fulfillment?
Requirements
What must be considered in the audit schedule regarding historical data?
The results of previous audits
What is the required relationship between the internal audit and the organization's established objectives?
The audit must ensure conformity with the established objectives
What mandatory document (4.6.3) outlines the specific areas and timing of the audit?
The audit plan and schedule
How is the internal audit process characterized in terms of methodology?
It is a systematic process
What must the audit verify regarding the organization's overall energy performance trend?
That the EnMS improves energy performance
What term describes the process of eliminating the cause of an audit finding that has not yet occurred (4.6.4)?
Preventive action
What does the evaluation of evidence allow the audit process to determine?
The extent to which requirements are fulfilled
If an organization combines its EnMS internal audit with its ISO 14001 internal audit, what must be defined to maintain clarity?
The intent and scope of each
What must be maintained and reported to top management concerning the internal audit?
Records of the audit results
What specific energy management tool (4.4.6) must the internal audit verify conformity with?
The energy objectives and targets
What must be true about the personnel who perform the internal audit concerning their skills?
They should be competent
What action must be taken concerning the audit schedule and plan?
It shall be developed
What two elements must be ensured regarding the integrity of the audit process?
Objectivity and impartiality
What must be documented regarding the audit's findings?
Records of the audit results
What management input (4.7.2) allows top management to assess the performance of the checking activities?
EnMS audit results [68 f)]
If an internal audit confirms that the EnMS is effectively implemented, what aspect of its maintenance must also be confirmed?
That it is effectively implemented and maintained
What is the general requirement for the frequency of internal audits?
They must be conducted at planned intervals
What must the organization ensure about the records of the internal audit?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained
When selecting an external person to perform an audit, what must the organization ensure about their competence?
The person should be competent
If the internal audit is conducted, what must be true about the process used to gather information?
It must be a documented process for obtaining evidence
What is the role of the internal audit concerning the overall effectiveness of the EnMS?
It ensures the EnMS is effectively implemented and maintained
What must be true about the audit results regarding their use by management?
They must be reported to top management
What specific input to the audit plan must be reviewed to avoid focusing too heavily on recently audited areas?
The results of previous audits
What must the organization develop to manage the audit process?
An audit plan and schedule
What type of action (4.6.4) is taken to correct a deficiency found by the internal audit?
Correction (action to eliminate a detected nonconformity)
If audit results highlight a potential nonconformity (4.6.4), what action must be considered to eliminate the cause before it occurs?
Preventive action
What characteristic of the processes being audited must be taken into consideration when developing the audit plan?
The status and importance of the processes
What must be maintained to demonstrate that the internal audit was performed?
Records of the audit results
If an organization uses the internal audit results as input for the Management Review (4.7.2), what specific item is being reviewed?
EnMS audit results [68 f)]
What specific element of planned arrangements must the internal audit verify conformity with, besides the requirements of the standard?
Planned arrangements for energy management
What term (3.20) implies that the audit must follow a defined method and sequence?
Systematic
What must be ensured about the internal audit regarding its scheduled occurrence?
It must be conducted at planned intervals
What must the internal audit verify concerning the organization's commitment to energy improvements (3.11)?
That the EnMS improves energy performance
What must be documented regarding the scope of the internal audit?
An audit plan and schedule shall be developed
What does the internal audit confirm about the organization's set of specified energy achievements (3.11)?
That the EnMS conforms with the energy objectives established
What must the internal audit verify about the integrity of the EnMS throughout the organization?
That it is effectively implemented and maintained
What level of management receives the final output of the internal audit?
Top management
What must the organization maintain concerning actions taken to eliminate potential audit findings?
Records of... preventive actions [66 e)]
When developing the audit plan, what aspect of previous audits must be considered?
The results of previous audits
What must the organization develop to formalize the internal audit process?
An audit plan and schedule
What does the internal audit provide management with that enables continual improvement?
The evidence that the EnMS improves energy performance
What information about the management review process must be maintained?
Records of the management review shall be maintained
What is an internal audit?

The internal audit, conducted at planned intervals, must ensure that the EnMS conforms to the planned arrangements for energy management, including the requirements of this International Standard. It must also confirm that the system conforms with the established energy objectives and targets, and that it is effectively implemented and maintained, leading to improved energy performance.
How to audit EnMS?

The internal audit, conducted at planned intervals, must ensure that the EnMS conforms to the planned arrangements for energy management, including the requirements of this International Standard. It must also confirm that the system conforms with the established energy objectives and targets, and that it is effectively implemented and maintained, leading to improved energy performance.
Audit checklist?

The internal audit, conducted at planned intervals, must ensure that the EnMS conforms to the planned arrangements for energy management, including the requirements of this International Standard. It must also confirm that the system conforms with the established energy objectives and targets, and that it is effectively implemented and maintained, leading to improved energy performance.
ask_management_responsibility: What is the fundamental commitment top management must demonstrate regarding the Energy Management System?

Top management must demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness . This involves several specific actions such as defining, establishing, implementing, and maintaining an energy policy, and ensuring that appropriate energy objectives and targets are established
How must top management ensure that energy performance is integrated into the organization’s long-term strategy?

Top management must ensure that energy performance is considered in long-term planning activities . Furthermore, organizations engaged in long-term planning can include considerations such as energy source, energy performance, and potential energy performance improvements during these planning activities
What specific responsibilities must the management representative fulfill, irrespective of any other duties they might have?

The appointed management representative, who must possess appropriate skills and competence, has the responsibility and authority to ensure the EnMS is established, implemented, maintained, and continually improved in accordance with the standard . They must also report to top management on both the overall performance of the EnMS and the energy performance results
What is the necessary composition and size of the energy management team?

The size and nature of the organization, along with the available resources, will determine the size of the team . For small organizations, the team may consist of just one person, possibly the management representative. For larger organizations, a cross-functional team provides an effective mechanism to engage different parts of the organization in the planning and implementation of the EnMS
Which specific resources must top management commit to providing for the Energy Management System?

Top management must provide the necessary resources to establish, implement, maintain, and improve the EnMS and the resulting energy performance . These resources include human resources, specialized skills, technology, and financial resources
What actions are required of top management to promote energy management awareness internally?

Top management is responsible for communicating the importance of energy management to everyone within the organization . They, or their representative, can support the importance of energy management through employee involvement activities, which include empowerment, motivation, recognition, training, and rewards, and participation
In addition to maintenance and improvement, what role does the management representative play in the planning phase of the Energy Management System?
The management representative must ensure that the planning of energy management activities is specifically designed to support the organization's
What is the general management obligation regarding the Energy Management System (EnMS)?
Top management shall demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness
What must top management ensure regarding the energy policy document?
Top management shall define, establish, implement and maintain an energy policy
What action must top management approve concerning internal resources for energy management?
Top management must approve the formation of an energy management team
What is the requirement for top management concerning resources for the EnMS?
Top management shall be providing the resources needed to establish, implement, maintain and improve the EnMS and the resulting energy performance
What physical or organizational boundary element must top management identify?
Top management shall be identifying the scope and boundaries to be addressed by the EnMS
What must top management communicate to those within the organization regarding energy stewardship?
Top management must be communicating the importance of energy management to those in the organization
What planning output must top management ensure is established?
Top management must be ensuring that energy objectives and targets are established
What must top management ensure regarding the suitability of measurement metrics?
Top management must be ensuring that EnPIs are appropriate to the organization
What specific time frame must top management incorporate energy performance into?
Top management must be considering energy performance in long-term planning
What two management activities related to checking and reporting must top management ensure?
Top management must be ensuring that results are measured and reported at determined intervals
What must successful implementation of the standard depend upon, concerning management engagement?
Successful implementation depends on commitment from all levels and functions of the organization, and especially from top management
What must the energy policy be appropriate to, concerning the organization's energy usage?
The energy policy shall be appropriate to the nature and scale of the organization's energy use and consumption
What commitment must the energy policy include regarding performance growth?
The policy must include a commitment to continual improvement in energy performance
What commitment must the energy policy include regarding the necessary support mechanisms?
The policy must include a commitment to ensure the availability of information and of necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
What commitment must the energy policy include regarding external compliance?
The policy must include a commitment to comply with applicable legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes related to its energy use, consumption and efficiency
What foundational role does the energy policy provide for planning goals?
The policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing energy objectives and targets
What specific procurement commitment must the energy policy support?
The policy supports the purchase of energy-efficient products and services
What two requirements must be fulfilled regarding the policy's documentation and internal presence?
The policy must be documented and communicated at all levels within the organization
What must be done to the energy policy at regular intervals?
The policy must be regularly reviewed, and updated as necessary
What is the specific instruction regarding the skills and competence of the appointed management representative(s)?
Top management shall appoint a management representative(s) with appropriate skills and competence
What must the management representative have, regardless of other duties?
The management representative has the responsibility and authority to fulfill their role
What is the management representative's primary responsibility regarding the integrity of the EnMS?
To ensure the EnMS is established, implemented, maintained, and continually improved in accordance with this International Standard
What must the management representative do concerning the energy management team?
The representative must identify person(s), authorized by an appropriate level of management, to work with the management representative in support of energy management activities
What specific performance report must the management representative provide to top management?
The representative must report to top management on energy performance
What specific system report must the management representative provide to top management?
The representative must report to top management on the performance of the EnMS
What link must the management representative ensure between planning activities and policy?
To ensure that the planning of energy management activities is designed to support the organization's energy policy
What must the management representative define and communicate to facilitate effective energy management?
The representative must define and communicate responsibilities and authorities in order to facilitate effective energy management
What must the management representative determine regarding the processes of the EnMS?
The representative must determine criteria and methods needed to ensure that both the operation and control of the EnMS are effective
What awareness promotion role does the management representative hold?
The representative must promote awareness of the energy policy and objectives at all levels of the organization
How does top management support the importance of energy management through personnel engagement?

Top management, or its representative, can support the importance of energy management through employee involvement activities such as empowerment, motivation, recognition, training and rewards and participation
What must be true about the management representative's responsibilities relative to their overall job function?
The responsibilities of the management representative may represent all or part of the job function
What factors determine the necessary skills and competencies of the management representative?
Skills and competencies can be determined as to an organization's size, culture and complexity, or to legal requirements or other requirements
What is the core function of the energy management team that top management approves?
The energy management team ensures delivery of energy performance improvements
What type of team structure provides an effective mechanism for larger organizations?
A cross-functional team provides an effective mechanism to engage different parts of the organization in the planning and implementation of the EnMS
What must top management ensure regarding the energy objectives and targets concerning their establishment?
Top management must be ensuring that energy objectives and targets are established
What must top management ensure is appropriate concerning the organization's measurement tools?
Top management must be ensuring that EnPIs are appropriate to the organization
What specific commitment must top management ensure the energy policy includes concerning resource availability?
Commitment to ensure the availability of information and of necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
What two concepts does top management ensure the energy policy provides a framework for?
The policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing energy objectives and energy targets
What must top management ensure regarding the policy's documentation and reach within the organization?
The policy must be documented and communicated at all levels within the organization
What organizational element defines the highest level of control and direction, according to the standard?
Top management is the person or group of people who directs and controls an organization at the highest level
Who controls the organization defined within the scope and boundaries of the EnMS?
Top management controls the organization defined within the scope and boundaries of the energy management system [34 Note 1]
What must the management representative report to top management regarding measurement results?
The representative must report to top management on energy performance
What must the management representative report to top management regarding system functionality?
The representative must report to top management on the performance of the EnMS
What must the energy policy state the organization's commitment to achieving?
The energy policy shall state the organization's commitment to achieving energy performance improvement
What must top management ensure about the energy policy regarding its periodic status?
The policy must be regularly reviewed, and updated as necessary
What two management actions related to long-term planning must top management consider, concerning energy performance?
Organizations conducting long-term planning can include considerations such as energy source, energy performance, and energy performance improvements in the planning activities
What must top management ensure regarding the energy policy's distribution?
The policy must be communicated at all levels within the organization
What must the management representative ensure about the EnMS activities in relation to the energy policy?
That the planning of energy management activities is designed to support the organization's energy policy
What must top management appoint, besides a management representative?
Top management must approve the formation of an energy management team
What must the management representative ensure regarding the establishment and maintenance of the EnMS?
The management representative must ensure the EnMS is established, implemented, maintained
What must top management ensure regarding the establishment of performance objectives?
Top management must ensure that energy objectives and targets are established
What action, in addition to providing training, must top management provide resources for?
Top management must provide the resources needed to establish, implement, maintain and improve the EnMS and the resulting energy performance
What is the requirement for top management concerning the organizational boundaries of the EnMS?
Top management must be identifying the scope and boundaries to be addressed by the EnMS
What commitment in the energy policy ensures support for the procurement phase?
The policy supports the purchase of energy-efficient products and services
What must the management representative determine to ensure the EnMS operation is effective?
Criteria and methods needed to ensure that both the operation and control of the EnMS are effective
What must the management review confirm regarding the EnMS's adequacy?
The review must ensure the EnMS's continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness
What is the management representative's role in relation to the management review?
The representative reports to top management on the performance of the EnMS and energy performance, which are inputs to the management review
What must the management representative report to top management regarding the system's output metrics?
The representative must report to top management on energy performance
How does top management ensure the planning process (4.4) is aligned with the organization's highest intentions?
By ensuring that energy objectives and targets are established , and that the policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing them
What organizational personnel must be identified to assist the management representative?
Person(s), authorized by an appropriate level of management, to work with the management representative
What must the management representative ensure about the operation and control of the EnMS?
To ensure that both the operation and control of the EnMS are effective
What must the management representative ensure regarding the consistency between the EnMS and the standard?
The representative must ensure the EnMS is established, implemented, maintained, and continually improved in accordance with this International Standard
What must the management representative ensure regarding the planning of EnMS activities?
The planning must be designed to support the organization's energy policy
What must top management ensure is maintained concerning the EnMS?
The organization must establish, document, implement, maintain and improve an EnMS
How do top management's commitment and resource provision enable continual improvement?
By providing the resources needed to establish, implement, maintain and improve the EnMS and the resulting energy performance
What specific output of the management review must address resource support?
Outputs shall include decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What must the management representative ensure regarding the definition of control methods?
The representative must determine criteria and methods needed to ensure effective control of the EnMS
What must top management ensure about the energy policy's ability to guide goal setting?
The policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing energy objectives and targets
What must be true about the management representative's authority compared to their other responsibilities?
The representative holds responsibility and authority irrespective of other responsibilities
What must top management ensure regarding the communication of energy management's importance?
It must be communicating the importance of energy management to those in the organization
What are management responsibilities?

Top management must demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness. This involves defining, establishing, implementing, and maintaining an energy policy, and ensuring that appropriate energy objectives and targets are established. Top management must provide necessary resources including human resources, specialized skills, technology, and financial resources.
Role of top management?

Top management must demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness. This involves defining, establishing, implementing, and maintaining an energy policy, and ensuring that appropriate energy objectives and targets are established. Top management must provide necessary resources including human resources, specialized skills, technology, and financial resources.
Management commitment?

Top management must demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness. This involves defining, establishing, implementing, and maintaining an energy policy, and ensuring that appropriate energy objectives and targets are established. Top management must provide necessary resources including human resources, specialized skills, technology, and financial resources.
ask_management_review: What is the primary purpose of top management reviewing the EnMS, and how often must this occur?
Top management shall review the organization's EnMS at planned intervals . The primary purpose is to ensure the system’s continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness
What information related to the ongoing management of the system must be included as input to the management review?

Inputs must include the follow-up actions from previous management reviews, the status of corrective actions and preventive actions, and the EnMS audit results . Recommendations for improvement should also be included
What are the required outputs concerning strategic policy and resource allocation from the management review?

The outputs from the management review must include any decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy . They must also include decisions regarding changes to the allocation of resources
What decisions regarding system goals and performance results must be included in the outputs of the management review?

The outputs must include any decisions or actions related to changes in the energy performance of the organization, and changes to the EnPIs . Crucially, decisions or actions related to changes to objectives, targets, or other elements of the EnMS must be documented, ensuring consistency with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What is the fundamental requirement for the organization concerning the Energy Management System?
The organization shall establish, document, implement, maintain and improve an EnMS in accordance with the requirements of this International Standard
What must the organization define and document regarding the extent of the EnMS?
The organization shall define and document the scope and boundaries of its EnMS
What goal must the organization determine how to meet regarding performance?
The organization must determine how it will meet the requirements of this International Standard in order to achieve continual improvement of its energy performance and of its EnMS
What framework is the EnMS model based upon?
The EnMS model is based on the Plan – Do – Check – Act (PDCA) continual improvement framework
What is the intended result of implementing an Energy Management System?
The implementation of an Energy Management System is intended to result in improved energy performance
What commitment must top management demonstrate regarding the EnMS?
Top management shall demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness
Whom must top management appoint concerning the EnMS structure?
Top management shall appoint a management representative and approve the formation of an energy management team
What four types of resources must top management commit to providing for the EnMS?
Resources include human resources, specialized skills, technology and financial resources
What must top management ensure regarding the setting of energy goals?
Top management shall ensure that energy objectives and targets are established
What must top management ensure is done with results data at determined intervals?
Top management shall ensure that results are measured and reported at determined intervals
What are the two key areas the management representative must report on to top management?
The management representative must report to top management on energy performance and the performance of the EnMS
What must the management representative determine regarding the operation and control of the EnMS?
The management representative must determine criteria and methods needed to ensure that both the operation and control of the EnMS are effective
What commitment must the policy include regarding necessary resources?
The policy must include a commitment to ensure the availability of information and of necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
What two types of external obligations must the energy policy commit to complying with?
The policy must commit to comply with applicable legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes
What function does the energy policy provide for setting goals?
The policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing energy objectives and targets
What must the energy policy support regarding purchasing and design?
The policy must support the purchase of energy-efficient products and services, and design for energy performance improvement
What must the energy planning process review regarding organizational actions?
Energy planning shall involve a review of the organization's activities that can affect energy performance
What must the organization identify, implement, and have access to regarding external requirements?
The organization shall identify, implement, and have access to the applicable legal requirements and other requirements related to its energy use, consumption and efficiency
What must the organization ensure about legal and other requirements when setting up the EnMS?
The organization shall ensure that these requirements are considered in establishing, implementing and maintaining the EnMS
What must the organization develop, record, and maintain for performance determination?
The organization shall develop, record, and maintain an energy review
What two types of data must the energy review analyze?
The energy review must analyse energy use and consumption based on measurement and other data
What specific facilities, equipment, or personnel must be identified based on the energy analysis?
The organization must identify the areas of significant energy use (SEU)
What must the organization do with opportunities for improving energy performance identified in the review?
The organization shall identify, prioritize and record opportunities for improving energy performance
When is the organization required to update the energy review?
The energy review shall be updated at defined intervals, as well as in response to major changes in facilities, equipment, systems, or processes
What shall the organization use to establish one or more energy baselines?
The organization shall establish an energy baseline(s) using the information in the initial energy review
Against what are changes in energy performance measured?
Changes in energy performance shall be measured against the energy baseline(s)
What must the organization identify for monitoring and measuring its energy performance?
The organization shall identify EnPIs appropriate for monitoring and measuring its energy performance
What must be recorded and regularly reviewed concerning EnPIs?
The methodology for determining and updating the EnPIs shall be recorded and regularly reviewed
What must be considered when establishing objectives and targets, besides legal requirements and significant energy use?
The organization shall also consider its financial, operational and business conditions, technological options and the views of interested parties
What must action plans include regarding responsibility and schedule?
Action plans shall include designation of responsibility and the means and time frame by which individual targets are to be achieved
What must the action plans state about performance improvement?
Action plans shall include a statement of the method by which an improvement in energy performance shall be verified
What is the general requirement for the implementation and operation phase?
The organization shall use the action plans and other outputs resulting from the planning process for
What is the basis for ensuring personnel competency related to significant energy uses?
Personnel shall be competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, skills or experience
What specific energy-related knowledge must personnel be aware of regarding their activities?
Personnel must be aware of the impact, actual or potential, with respect to energy use and consumption, of their activities
What must the organization establish to receive suggestions from personnel?
The organization shall establish and implement a process by which any person working for, or on behalf of, the organization can make comments or suggest improvements to the EnMS
What must the organization decide and document regarding external communication?
The organization shall decide whether to communicate externally about its
What must the EnMS documentation describe concerning the system's elements?
The documentation shall describe the core elements of the EnMS and their interaction
What is one factor that can cause the degree of documentation to vary between organizations?
The degree of documentation can vary based on the scale of the organization and type of activities, the complexity of the processes and their interactions, or the competence of personnel
What must be established for documents before they are issued?
The organization shall establish procedures to approve documents for adequacy prior to issue
What must be done to ensure documents are up-to-date at all times?
The organization must ensure that changes and the current revision status of documents are identified
What must the organization prevent regarding retired documents?
The organization must prevent the unintended use of obsolete documents
What is the purpose of identifying and planning operations related to significant energy uses?
The purpose is to ensure that operations are carried out under specified conditions, consistent with the
When must criteria be established for the effective operation and maintenance of significant energy uses?
Criteria must be established where their absence could lead to a significant deviation from effective energy performance
What must the organization consider during the design of new or modified facilities and equipment?
The organization shall consider energy performance improvement opportunities and operational control in the design
What information must the organization provide to suppliers when procuring energy-impacting items?
The organization shall inform suppliers that procurement is partly evaluated on the basis of energy performance
What criteria must be established when procuring energy-using products concerning time?
Criteria must be established for assessing energy use, consumption and efficiency over the planned or expected operating lifetime
What is procurement considered regarding energy performance?
Procurement is an opportunity to improve energy performance through the use of more efficient products and services
What must the organization monitor, measure, and analyze at planned intervals?
The organization shall ensure that the key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance are monitored, measured and analysed
What must be monitored regarding action plans?
The effectiveness of the action plans in achieving objectives and targets must be monitored
What comparison must be monitored, measured, and analyzed?
The evaluation of actual versus expected energy consumption must be monitored
What must be defined and implemented regarding energy measurement?
An energy measurement plan, appropriate to the size and complexity of the organization, shall be defined and implemented
What two qualities of data must the monitoring equipment provide?
The equipment must provide data which are accurate and repeatable
What must be maintained to establish the accuracy of measurement equipment?
Records of calibration and other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability shall be maintained
What action must the organization take regarding significant deviations in energy performance?
The organization shall investigate and respond to significant deviations in energy performance
What must be maintained regarding the results of compliance evaluation?
Records of the results of the evaluations of compliance shall be maintained
What three areas must the internal audit ensure the EnMS conforms to?
The audit must ensure the EnMS conforms to planned arrangements, established energy objectives and targets, and the requirements of this International Standard
What two factors must the audit plan and schedule take into consideration?
The plan must consider the status and importance of the processes and areas to be audited as well as the results of previous audits
What two qualities must the selection and conduct of auditors ensure?
The process shall ensure objectivity and impartiality of the audit process
What must be done with the records of the audit results?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained and reported to top management
What must be determined regarding the causes of problems?
The organization must determine the causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What must be maintained regarding corrective and preventive actions?
Records of corrective actions and preventive actions shall be maintained
What relationship must exist between the corrective actions and the problems encountered?
Corrective actions and preventive actions shall be appropriate to the magnitude of the actual or potential problems and the energy performance consequences encountered
What must the organization ensure is done to the EnMS after implementing necessary actions?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What is the purpose of maintaining records in the EnMS?
Records are necessary to demonstrate conformity to the requirements of its EnMS and of this International Standard, and the energy performance results achieved
What three qualities must records maintain?
Records shall be and shall remain legible, identifiable and traceable to the relevant activity
How often must top management review the EnMS?
Top management shall review the organization's EnMS at planned intervals
What three qualities must the management review ensure the EnMS possesses?
The review must ensure the EnMS's continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness
What must be reviewed regarding the achievement of system goals?
The review must include the extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
What future-looking input must be included in the review?
Projected energy performance for the following period must be included as input
What status reports must be included as inputs to the review?
EnMS audit results and the status of corrective actions and preventive actions must be included
What decisions must the outputs from the management review include regarding policy and EnPIs?
Outputs shall include decisions or actions related to changes to the
What type of changes must the outputs relate to regarding resources?
Outputs shall include decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What must outputs concerning changes to objectives and targets be consistent with?
Changes must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What is the purpose of Annex A in relation to the requirements of Clause Four?
Annex A provides informative guidance to prevent misinterpretation of the requirements contained in Clause 4
Can all elements of the EnMS be reviewed at once during a management review?
No, not all elements of the EnMS need to be reviewed at once, and the review process may take place over a period of time
What overall goal must the organization determine how to achieve by meeting the standard's requirements?
The organization must determine how it will meet the requirements in order to achieve continual improvement of its energy performance and of its EnMS
Whom must top management appoint and what team must they approve the formation of?
Top management shall appoint a management representative and approve the formation of an energy management team
What must top management ensure is established regarding energy goals?
Top management shall ensure that energy objectives and targets are established
What must top management ensure regarding Energy Performance Indicators (EnPIs)?
Top management shall ensure that EnPIs are appropriate to the organization
What must top management consider regarding energy performance in long-term planning?
Top management must consider energy performance in long-term planning activities
What must the management representative define and communicate to facilitate effective energy management?
The management representative must define and communicate responsibilities and authorities in order to facilitate effective energy management
What must the management representative ensure regarding the planning of energy management activities?
The management representative must ensure that the planning of energy management activities is designed to support the organization's
What commitment must the policy include regarding necessary resources and information?
The policy must include a commitment to ensure the availability of information and of necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
What must the energy policy support regarding purchasing?
The policy must support the purchase of energy-efficient products and services
What must energy planning lead to regarding the organization's performance?
Energy planning shall lead to activities that continually improve energy performance
What must the organization identify and have access to regarding external energy requirements?
The organization shall identify, implement, and have access to the applicable legal requirements and other requirements related to its energy use, consumption and efficiency
How often must legal and other requirements be reviewed?
Legal requirements and other requirements shall be reviewed at defined intervals
What must the organization develop, record, and maintain to determine energy performance?
The organization shall develop, record, and maintain an energy review
What must be documented regarding the energy review process itself?
The methodology and criteria used to develop the energy review shall be documented
What must the analysis identify regarding consumption areas?
The analysis must identify the areas of significant energy use (SEU)
What must be identified regarding personnel in relation to significant energy use?
The review must identify personnel working for, or on behalf of, the organization that significantly affect energy use and consumption
What three actions must the organization take regarding opportunities for improvement identified in the review?
The organization shall identify, prioritize and record opportunities for improving energy performance
When is the organization required to update the energy review in response to facility changes?
The energy review shall be updated in response to major changes in facilities, equipment, systems, or processes
What is the purpose of establishing an energy baseline?
The energy baseline provides a reference against which changes in energy performance shall be measured
What must be considered when establishing the energy baseline regarding time?
The organization shall consider a data period suitable to the organization's energy use and consumption
What must be recorded and regularly reviewed concerning EnPI methodology?
The methodology for determining and updating the EnPIs shall be recorded and regularly reviewed
How should EnPIs be used in relation to the energy baseline?
EnPIs shall be reviewed and compared to the energy baseline as appropriate
What must the organization establish, implement, and maintain at relevant functions and levels?
The organization shall establish, implement and maintain documented energy objectives and targets
What must be established for achievement of objectives and targets?
Time frames shall be established for achievement of the objectives and targets
What must action plans specify regarding schedule and means?
Action plans shall include the means and time frame by which individual targets are to be achieved
What statement must action plans include regarding performance verification?
Action plans shall include a statement of the method by which an improvement in energy performance shall be verified
How often must action plans be updated?
The action plans shall be documented, and updated at defined intervals
What must the organization use for the implementation and operation phase?
The organization shall use the action plans and other outputs resulting from the planning process
What must the organization maintain regarding personnel development?
Appropriate records of training and other actions shall be maintained
What must personnel be aware of regarding the EnMS procedures?
Personnel must be aware of the importance of conformity with the
What must personnel be aware of regarding performance?
Personnel must be aware of the benefits of improved energy performance
What process must be established to receive input from personnel?
The organization shall establish and implement a process by which any person working for, or on behalf of, the organization can make comments or suggest improvements to the EnMS
What must the organization decide and document regarding communication outside the organization?
The organization shall decide whether to communicate externally about its
In what media can the EnMS documentation be maintained?
Documentation can be maintained in paper, electronic or any other medium
What must be done to ensure documents are adequate before being issued?
The organization shall establish procedures to approve documents for adequacy prior to issue
What must be done periodically to ensure documents remain current?
The organization shall periodically review and update documents as necessary
What must the organization ensure regarding the availability of documents?
The organization shall ensure that relevant versions of applicable documents are available at points of use
What must be done to ensure obsolete documents are managed correctly?
The organization must prevent the unintended use of obsolete documents, and suitably identify those to be retained for any purpose
What must be ensured about operations related to significant energy use?
They must be carried out under specified conditions, consistent with the
What must the organization consider in the design of new facilities and equipment?
The organization shall consider energy performance improvement opportunities and operational control in the design
What must be incorporated into project specifications and procurement activities?
The results of the energy performance evaluation shall be incorporated where appropriate into the specification, design and procurement activities
What must the organization inform suppliers of regarding procurement evaluation?
The organization shall inform suppliers that procurement is partly evaluated on the basis of energy performance
What must the organization establish criteria for when procuring energy-using products?
The criteria must assess energy use, consumption and efficiency over the planned or expected operating lifetime
What must be monitored, measured, and analyzed at planned intervals?
The key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance shall be monitored, measured and analysed
What key characteristic related to goals must be monitored?
The effectiveness of the action plans in achieving objectives and targets must be monitored
What must the internal audit ensure regarding the system's effectiveness?
The audit must ensure the EnMS is effectively implemented and maintained, and improves energy performance
What must the audit plan take into consideration besides the status of processes?
The audit plan must consider the results of previous audits
What must the organization address regarding requirement failures?
The organization shall address actual and potential nonconformities by making corrections, and by taking corrective action and preventive action
What is a necessary action after reviewing a nonconformity?
The organization must determine the causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What must be true about the magnitude of the corrective and preventive actions taken?
Actions shall be appropriate to the magnitude of the actual or potential problems and the energy performance consequences encountered
What control must be implemented for records regarding their management lifecycle?
The organization shall define and implement controls for the identification, retrieval and retention of records
What three qualities must records maintain for traceability?
Records shall be and shall remain legible, identifiable and traceable to the relevant activity
What performance data input must be included in the management review?
A review of energy performance and related EnPIs must be included
What decisions must the outputs include regarding changes to objectives and targets?
Outputs shall include decisions or actions related to changes to objectives, targets or other elements of the EnMS
What decisions must the outputs include regarding resources?
Outputs shall include decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What are the required inputs concerning compliance and legal matters for the management review?

The inputs to the management review must include a review of the results of the evaluation of compliance with legal requirements . They must also include any identified changes in legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes
What specific performance data must be analyzed as part of the management review input?

The management review input must include a review of energy performance and related EnPIs . Additionally, the review must assess the extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met, and include the projected energy performance for the following period
What three qualities must the management review ensure about the EnMS?
The review must ensure the EnMS's continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness
In the context of the PDCA cycle, which phase does the management review fall under?
The management review is part of the Act phase, which leads to continual improvement
If the review process is conducted over an extended period, what is the expectation regarding the coverage of the management system?
The management review should cover the scope of the energy management system
Does the standard require all elements of the energy management system to be reviewed simultaneously?
No, not all elements of the energy management system need to be reviewed at once
If the management review takes place over a period of time, what must still be achieved?
The review process may take place over a period of time, but it should still cover the scope of the energy management system
What two concepts related to quantifiable measurement must be reviewed as input?
Review of energy performance and related EnPIs (Energy Performance Indicators)
What input ensures that the organization assesses its adherence to external obligations?
Results of the evaluation of compliance with legal requirements and other requirements
What change information related to compliance must be included in the review input?
Changes in legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes
What specific input measures the success of the Planning phase outputs?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
If there is a change in national energy conservation law, what management review input covers this development?
Changes in legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes
What input assesses the financial return and operational impact of past energy goals?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
What mandatory output of the management review relates to the organization's achieved energy results?
Decisions or actions related to changes in the energy performance of the organization
What mandatory output relates to the foundational document of the EnMS?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy
What mandatory output ensures that planning goals are updated after the review?
Decisions or actions related to changes to objectives, targets or other elements of the EnMS
What specific commitment must the changes to objectives, targets, or other elements of the EnMS be consistent with?
Changes must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What crucial resource allocation decision is a required output of the management review?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
If the management review determines that the organization's energy policy is no longer appropriate to its scale of use, what output action is required?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy
What is the required consequence of the review concerning the organization's financial support for the EnMS?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
How does the management review output reinforce the organization's overall strategic goal for the EnMS?
Outputs must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
If the internal audit results show a systemic failure in the operational control procedures, what output action must result regarding the EnMS framework?
Decisions or actions related to changes to... other elements of the EnMS
What must be verified regarding energy objectives and targets during the management review?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
If the management review decides to implement a major redesign of a significant energy-using process, what output category covers the financial approval for this?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What input confirms the compliance status regarding non-mandatory agreements (e.g., voluntary codes)?
Results of the evaluation of compliance with... other requirements to which the organization subscribes
If top management makes decisions about changes to targets, what requirement guides these changes?
Changes must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
If the review identifies that the organization's energy performance has been excellent, what output action might result regarding the EnMS goals?
Decisions or actions related to changes to objectives, targets or other elements of the EnMS
What is the key difference between the input 'Review of energy policy' and the output 'Changes to the energy policy'?
The input is the assessment of the existing policy, while the output is the decision or action to modify it
When reviewing compliance, what must the input report include in addition to the evaluation results?
Changes in legal requirements and other requirements to which the organization subscribes
If the review determines that a major resource adjustment is needed, what must be documented as the output?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What management input is crucial for assessing the performance of the 'Do' phase of the EnMS?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
What must top management ensure regarding the objectives and targets as a follow-up action from the review?
They must ensure that energy objectives and targets are established [36 f)], potentially through decisions made in the management review output
If the review determines a shift in the organization's overall direction, what output must reflect this shift?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy
What is the ultimate purpose of management review concerning the EnMS model?
To ensure the systematic approach in achieving continual improvement of energy performance and the EnMS
If the review assesses compliance with voluntary industry codes, under which input category is this included?
Results of the evaluation of compliance with... other requirements to which the organization subscribes
What must the organization report on regarding its energy objectives during the management review?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
How is the concept of 'continual improvement' integrated into the mandatory outputs of the management review?
Outputs related to changes in objectives, targets, or other elements must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What must the review confirm about the EnMS's ability to cover the defined energy boundaries?
Its continuing adequacy (sufficiency to meet defined scope and boundaries)
If the organization determines external communication (4.5.3) is necessary, how is this decision potentially addressed as a management review output?
It may be addressed as a change to other elements of the EnMS or a change to allocation of resources
What mandatory output relates to the quantifiable achievement of the organization's energy goals?
Decisions or actions related to changes in the energy performance of the organization
What input covers the outcomes of the Checking phase (4.6) activities overall?
Multiple inputs cover the Checking phase, including audit results, status of actions, and compliance evaluation results
If the review determines that external communication of the EnMS should begin, what output covers the assignment of funds for this?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What mandatory output ensures that necessary changes are made to the management system components beyond objectives and targets?
Decisions or actions related to changes to... other elements of the EnMS
If a significant change is made to the energy policy as a result of the review, what mandatory output records this decision?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy
What mandatory output directly addresses the organization's commitment to energy improvements (3.2)?
