The energy review shall be updated at defined intervals, as well as in response to major changes in facilities, equipment, systems, or processes
What is the specific requirement for the energy policy regarding its documentation and communication?
The energy policy must be documented and communicated at all levels within the organization
What concept in the standard provides organizations with flexibility when defining what is included in their Energy Management System?

The concept of scope and boundaries allows the organization flexibility in defining what is included within the Energy Management System . The scope defines the extent of activities, facilities, and decisions, while the boundaries define the physical or organizational limits
What are some examples of activities an organization might undertake to improve energy performance, beyond simple reduction in consumption?

Since energy performance encompasses energy efficiency and use, the organization can choose from a wide range of activities . For instance, an organization could focus on reducing peak demand, utilizing surplus or waste energy, or improving the operations of its systems, processes, or equipment
What are examples of personnel considered to be "working on behalf of the organization" in the context of the energy review?
Examples of personnel working on behalf of the organization include service contractors, part-time personnel, and temporary staff
What is the implication for two different organizations operating similar processes but having different energy performances, regarding conformity?

This International Standard does not establish absolute requirements for energy performance . Consequently, two organizations carrying out similar operations, but having different energy performance, can both conform to its requirements
What is the organization required to consider when designing its measurement methodology?

The organization must define and implement an energy measurement plan and must also define and periodically review its measurement needs . It is the responsibility of the organization to determine the means and methods of measurement, which may range from simple utility meters to complex monitoring systems
What must the organization do concerning its energy management action plans at defined intervals?
The energy management action plans, established for achieving objectives and targets, shall be documented, and updated at defined intervals
In the context of energy management, what does "normalizing" the energy baseline involve?

Normalizing an energy baseline involves using variables which affect energy use and/or consumption to adjust the baseline . Examples of such variables include production level or degree days (outdoor temperature)
What is the goal of incorporating energy performance evaluation results into project specifications?

The goal is to ensure that the results of the energy performance evaluation are appropriately incorporated into the specification, design, and procurement activities of new, modified, or renovated projects
What is the general relationship between energy consumption and energy efficiency as components of energy performance?

Energy performance is the measurable result related to energy consumption and energy efficiency . Energy efficiency is a quantitative relationship between an output and an input of energy, while energy consumption is the quantity of energy applied
Define the concept of "continual improvement" within the context of the Energy Management System.

Continual improvement is defined as a recurring process that results in the enhancement of both the energy performance and the overall Energy Management System (EnMS) . This process involves continually establishing objectives and finding opportunities for enhancement. Crucially, continual improvement achieves better overall energy performance consistent with the intentions laid out in the organization's energy policy
Explain the role and characteristics of the "energy baseline" in energy management.

The energy baseline serves as a quantitative reference or set of references that provides a basis for comparing and measuring energy performance . This baseline reflects a specified period of time and may be adjusted or normalized using variables that affect energy consumption, such as the production level or outdoor temperature (degree days). The baseline is critically important because it is used as a reference point both before and after energy performance improvement actions are implemented, allowing for the calculation of energy savings
What is "energy efficiency," and how is it measured?

Energy efficiency is defined as a ratio or another quantitative relationship established between an output—which could be performance, a service, goods, or energy—and an input of energy . Examples include conversion efficiency or the ratio of output to input. To be measurable, both the input and the output quantities and qualities must be clearly specified
Describe the composition and responsibility of the "energy management team."

The energy management team consists of the person or persons responsible for the effective implementation of the Energy Management System activities and for delivering improvements in energy performance . The size of this team is flexible, being determined by the available resources and the size and nature of the organization; it may even be constituted by a single person, such as the management representative
What is the definition of "energy performance," and what is the relationship between energy performance and the EnMS?

Energy performance refers to the measurable results related to energy efficiency, energy use, and energy consumption . In the context of an Energy Management System, these results are measured against the organization's energy policy, established objectives, targets, and other energy performance requirements. Energy performance is considered one component of the overall performance of the Energy Management System
What information must a "record" contain, and what is its use?

A record is a document that either states the results achieved or provides evidence of activities performed . Records are used, for example, to provide evidence of verification, traceability, preventive action, and corrective action
What is "energy use," and what are some examples of activities that fall under this definition?
Energy use is defined as the manner or kind of application of energy . Examples of energy use include ventilation, lighting, heating, cooling, transportation, processes, and production lines
What are the four actions an organization must undertake concerning the Energy Management System according to the General Requirements?
The organization shall undertake four key actions: it must establish, document, implement, maintain, and continually improve an EnMS in accordance with the requirements of this International Standard
What core elements must the organization define and document before implementing the system?

Before implementation, the organization must define and document the scope and boundaries of its Energy Management System . The scope details the extent of activities, facilities, and decisions addressed by the EnMS, while the boundaries define the physical or organizational limits
Describe the process known as "continual improvement."
Continual improvement is a recurring process which results in enhancement of energy performance and the energy management system
What is the definition of a "correction" in relation to a detected requirement failure?
A correction is the specific action to eliminate a detected nonconformity
What is the relationship between corrective action and preventive action regarding their intended purpose?
Corrective action is taken to prevent recurrence of a nonconformity, whereas preventive action is taken to prevent occurrence
What is the precise definition of "energy consumption?"
Energy consumption is defined as the quantity of energy applied
How is "energy efficiency" fundamentally defined, referencing inputs and outputs?
Energy efficiency is defined as a ratio or other quantitative relationship between an output of performance, service, goods or energy, and an input of energy
What are two examples of an energy efficiency ratio given in the standard?
Examples include conversion efficiency and the ratio of output divided by input
Who constitutes the "energy management team," and what is their responsibility?
The energy management team consists of the person(s) responsible for the effective implementation of the EnMS activities and for delivering energy performance improvements
What is the relationship between energy performance and the performance of the overall EnMS?
Energy performance is one component of the performance of the energy management system
What are "energy services?"
Energy services are defined as the activities and their results related to the provision and/or use of energy
What is the definition of "energy use?"
Energy use is defined as the manner or kind of application of energy
What is an "interested party" regarding the organization’s energy performance?
An interested party is any person or group concerned with, or affected by, the energy performance of the organization
Describe the characteristics of an "organization" as defined in the standard.
An organization is an entity, whether incorporated or not, public or private, that has its own functions and administration and that has the authority to control its energy use and consumption
What is the aim of "preventive action" regarding nonconformities?
Preventive action is the action to eliminate the cause of a potential nonconformity
What is a "procedure," and must it be documented?

A procedure is a specified way to carry out an activity or a process. Procedures can be documented or not, though "written procedure" or "documented procedure" is frequently used when it is documented
What is a "record," and what two types of information does it contain?
A record is a document stating results achieved or providing evidence of activities performed
Who is defined as "top management?"
Top management is the person or group of people who directs and controls an organization at the highest level
What is the difference in purpose between a record and a general document in the EnMS?
A record documents results achieved or provides evidence of activities performed , while general documentation is required to describe the core elements of the EnMS and their interaction
What characteristics define a person or group as "Top Management"?
They are the person or group of people who directs and controls an organization at the highest level
What are three examples of "energy use" cited in the sources?
Examples of energy use include ventilation, lighting, heating, cooling, transportation, processes, and production lines
What is the definition of "energy use"?
Energy use is defined as the manner or kind of application of energy
What is the relationship between the organization's commitment and the continual improvement process rate?
The organization is given flexibility in how it implements the EnMS; the rate, extent and timescale of the continual improvement process are determined by the organization
What three concepts are encompassed within the broad term "energy performance"?

The concept of energy performance includes three key aspects: energy use, energy efficiency, and energy consumption . This allows the organization a wide range of activities through which to demonstrate performance improvement
Who determines the pace of continual improvement for the Energy Management System?

The organization determines the rate, extent, and timescale of the continual improvement process . The organization has the flexibility to take into account economic and other considerations when making these determinations
ask_benchmarking: How is benchmarking defined, and what value does it offer to the energy planning process?

Benchmarking is defined as the process of collecting, analyzing, and relating energy performance data of comparable activities to evaluate and compare performance either between or within entities . Provided that relevant and accurate data are available, benchmarking offers a valuable input to the objective energy review and the subsequent setting of energy objectives and targets
What are the different types of benchmarking, and what is the goal of external benchmarking?

Different types of benchmarking exist, including internal benchmarking, which aims to highlight good practices within the organization . External benchmarking aims to establish the "best in industry/sector" performance for an installation, facility, product, or service within the same field or sector
What is the role of benchmarking as an input to the energy planning process?
Provided relevant and accurate data are available, benchmarking is a valuable input to an objective energy review and consequent setting of energy objectives and targets
ask_pdca: How is the Plan-Do-Check-Act (PDCA) model applied to energy management within the context of this International Standard?

The International Standard is fundamentally based on the Plan - Do - Check - Act (PDCA) framework for continual improvement . Plan involves conducting the energy review, establishing the energy baseline, EnPIs, objectives, targets, and action plans necessary to achieve results consistent with the energy policy. Do involves implementing the energy management action plans. Check requires monitoring and measuring processes and key operational characteristics against the policy and objectives, and reporting the results. Act requires taking actions to continually improve both energy performance and the EnMS
process: What is the fundamental requirement the organization must establish concerning energy reference points?
The organization shall establish an energy baseline(s)
What is the official definition of an energy baseline (EnB)?
An EnB is a quantitative reference(s) providing a basis for comparison of energy performance
What must the energy baseline reflect regarding time?
An energy baseline reflects a specified period of time
What specific type of reference point does the EnB provide?
A quantitative reference(s)
What does the EnB provide a basis for comparing?
Energy performance
What must be measured against the established energy baseline(s)?
Changes in energy performance shall be measured against the energy baseline(s)
What information source must be used when establishing the energy baseline(s)?
The organization shall establish an energy baseline(s) using the information in the initial energy review
What must the organization consider regarding time when establishing the EnB?
The organization must consider a data period suitable to the organization's energy use and consumption
What is the EnB used for when quantifying results related to improvement actions?
The energy baseline is used for calculation of energy savings
The EnB is used as a reference point both before and after the implementation of what specific actions?
Energy performance improvement actions
What are the two mandatory administrative actions required for the energy baseline(s) after establishment?
The energy baseline(s) shall be maintained and recorded
If Energy Performance Indicators (EnPIs) no longer reflect organizational energy use, what action is required regarding the baseline?
Adjustments to the baseline(s) shall be made
If EnPIs no longer reflect organizational energy consumption, what action is required regarding the baseline?
Adjustments to the baseline(s) shall be made
What type of change related to organizational systems triggers a mandatory baseline adjustment?
Major changes to the energy systems
What type of change related to organizational processes triggers a mandatory baseline adjustment?
Major changes to the process
What type of change related to organizational activities triggers a mandatory baseline adjustment?
Major changes to operational patterns
If not triggered by physical changes, adjustments to the baseline may be made based on what procedural rule?
According to a predetermined method
What quantitative measure must be reviewed and compared to the energy baseline, as appropriate?
EnPIs (Energy Performance Indicators) shall be reviewed and compared to the energy baseline
What administrative purpose does the maintained and recorded EnB serve regarding documentation?
The EnB is maintained and recorded as a means for the organization to determine the records maintenance period
Are the adjustments made to the baseline considered a part of maintenance requirements?
Yes, the adjustments to the baseline are also considered maintenance
The EnB can be normalized using variables which affect what first component of energy performance?
Variables which affect energy use
The EnB can be normalized using variables which affect what second component of energy performance?
Variables which affect energy consumption
What specific variable related to production level is cited as an example for normalization?
Production level
What specific variable related to outdoor conditions is cited as an example for normalization?
Degree days (outdoor temperature)
In the context of normalization, what specific weather variable must a suitable data period account for?
Weather
In the context of normalization, what specific seasonal variable must a suitable data period account for?
Seasons
In the context of normalization, what specific variable related to organizational timing must a suitable data period account for?
Business activity cycles
What type of requirements must a suitable data period account for, in addition to variables?
Regulatory requirements
What must the organization define in advance to allow adjustments based on a pre-set rule?
A predetermined method

The importance of conformity with the procedures
What must the organization define to implement the competence requirements?
Competence, training and awareness requirements
What must be ensured regarding the documentation (records) of the competence process?
Records shall be appropriate and maintained
The awareness requirement states personnel must know how their activities and behavior contribute to the achievement of what?
Energy objectives and targets
What must the organization identify and address regarding capability gaps in controlling significant energy uses?
Training needs
What is the mandatory action if a training need is identified?
Provide training or take other actions to meet these needs
What must the organization ensure regarding the personnel working on its behalf who are related to SEUs?
That they are competent
What organizational needs determine the specific requirements for competence and training?
The organizational needs
What must personnel be aware of concerning the requirements of the EnMS?
The importance of conformity with the requirements of the EnMS
What two elements related to duties must personnel be aware of in achieving EnMS requirements?
Their roles and responsibilities
If an organization utilizes part-time personnel related to SEUs, what must be ensured about them?
That they are competent
What type of records must be maintained regarding the training provided?
Appropriate records
The management representative's competence must be appropriate to what two potential requirements?
Legal requirements or other requirements
What must personnel be aware of concerning their activities and energy use/consumption?
The impact, actual or potential, of their activities
What action must the organization take if training needs are identified?
Provide training
What must the organization use to identify the need for training actions?
The needs associated with the control of its significant energy uses and the operation of its EnMS
What must the organization ensure is based on a relevant combination of education, training, skills, and experience?
Competency
What must personnel be aware of regarding deviations from specified procedures?
The potential consequences of departure from specified procedures
What is the fundamental obligation Top Management must demonstrate regarding the Energy Management System (EnMS)?
Top management shall demonstrate its commitment to support the EnMS and to continually improve its effectiveness
What specific action must Top Management take regarding the organization's Energy Policy?
Top management shall define, establish, implement and maintain an energy policy
What key personnel role must Top Management appoint to oversee the EnMS execution?
Top management shall appoint a management representative
What collective structure related to energy management must Top Management formally sanction?
Top management must approve the formation of an energy management team
What general requirement must Top Management provide to enable the establishment, implementation, maintenance, and improvement of the EnMS?
Top management must provide the resources needed
What specific type of non-human resource is mentioned that Top Management must provide?
Top management must provide technology
What specific organizational limits must Top Management be responsible for identifying?
Top management must be responsible for identifying the scope and boundaries to be addressed by the EnMS
What action must Top Management take regarding the importance of the EnMS to the organization's personnel?
Top management must communicate the importance of energy management to those in the organization
What specific performance goals must Top Management ensure are set within the organization?
Top management shall ensure that energy objectives and targets are established
What must Top Management ensure regarding the organization's Energy Performance Indicators (EnPIs)?
Top management shall ensure that EnPIs are appropriate to the organization
What organizational activity, relating to future decisions, must Top Management integrate energy performance into?
Top management must consider energy performance in long-term planning
What must Top Management ensure happens to performance results at specified timeframes?
Top management shall ensure that results are measured and reported at determined intervals
What formal review process must Top Management conduct at determined intervals?
Top management shall be responsible for conducting management reviews
What commitment must Top Management demonstrate toward the ongoing success of the EnMS?
A commitment to continually improve its effectiveness
What commitment must Top Management ensure is stated within the energy policy regarding performance?
The energy policy shall state the organization's commitment to achieving energy performance improvement
What must Top Management ensure regarding the Energy Policy in terms of its fit with the organization?
Top management must ensure the policy is appropriate to the nature and scale of the organization's energy use and consumption
What resource, related to expertise, must Top Management ensure is provided?
Top management must provide specialized skills
What resource, related to manpower, must Top Management ensure is provided?
Top management must provide human resources
What resource, related to money, must Top Management ensure is provided?
Top management must provide financial resources
In addition to the management representative, who else must Top Management approve the creation of?
The energy management team
What authority must Top Management give the management representative, irrespective of their other duties?
The responsibility and authority to ensure the EnMS is established, implemented, maintained, and continually improved in accordance with the standard
What specific performance data must the management representative report back to Top Management on?
The management representative must report to top management on energy performance
What specific system performance data must the management representative report back to Top Management on?
The management representative must report to top management on the performance of the EnMS
What must Top Management ensure regarding the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the EnMS during the management review?
Top management shall review the EnMS to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness
What specific management action ensures that organizational limits are clearly defined for the EnMS?
Top management is responsible for identifying the scope and boundaries to be addressed by the EnMS
What must Top Management ensure is established for the management representative besides authority?
That the management representative is appointed with appropriate skills and competence
What must Top Management ensure is established regarding energy objectives?
Ensuring that energy objectives and targets are established
What must Top Management ensure is provided so that objectives and targets can be met?
Top management must commit to ensure the availability of necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
When communicating the importance of energy management, how can Top Management support this concept within the organization?
Through employee involvement activities
Name one example of an employee involvement activity that Top Management can use to support energy management communication.
Empowerment, motivation, recognition, training or rewards and participation
What specific type of review must Top Management ensure is conducted at planned intervals?
Management review
What organizational element does Top Management direct and control, which is defined within the scope and boundaries?
The organization defined within the scope and boundaries of the energy management system
Top Management must ensure the energy policy is regularly managed in what two ways?
It is regularly reviewed, and updated as necessary
What mandatory output from the management review must include decisions or actions related to organizational finance?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What mandatory output from the management review must include decisions or actions related to the foundational energy document?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the energy policy
What mandatory output from the management review must include decisions or actions related to the measurement metrics?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the EnPIs
What aspect of the continual improvement process must the outputs from the management review be consistent with?
Changes to objectives, targets or other elements of the EnMS must be consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What is the overall goal of the organization's commitment that Top Management must demonstrate support for?
To continually improve its energy performance and the EnMS
Top Management must ensure the policy supports the design of new projects for what purpose?
To support design for energy performance improvement
Top Management must ensure the policy supports the procurement of what kind of products?
The purchase of energy-efficient products and services
What must Top Management ensure is identified when conducting long-term planning regarding energy sources?
Energy source
What three types of resources must Top Management provide to establish, implement, maintain, and improve the EnMS?
Human resources, specialized skills, technology and financial resources
What specific information about the EnMS must Top Management ensure is communicated at all levels within the organization?
The energy policy
Top Management must ensure the policy provides the framework for setting and reviewing what two specific planning elements?
Energy objectives and targets
What must Top Management ensure is established regarding the management review records?
Records of management review shall be maintained
What must Top Management ensure regarding the audit results of the EnMS?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained and reported to top management
What specific input related to future performance must be included in the management review conducted by Top Management?
Projected energy performance for the following period
What specific input related to past performance must be included in the management review conducted by Top Management?
The extent to which the energy objectives and targets have been met
What specific input related to checking actions must be included in the management review conducted by Top Management?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
What specific input related to external factors must be included in the management review conducted by Top Management?
Results of the evaluation of compliance with legal requirements and changes in legal requirements and other requirements
Top Management must ensure that the EnPIs are appropriate to the organization, particularly for what purpose?
To monitor and measure its energy performance
What must Top Management ensure regarding the energy management activities planned by the management representative?
Ensure that the planning of energy management activities is designed to support the organization's energy policy
What general requirement must Top Management ensure the organization meets to achieve continual improvement?
Determine how it will meet the requirements of this International Standard
Top Management must ensure the policy is what, in terms of documentation and availability?
Documented and communicated at all levels within the organization
What must Top Management consider in long-term planning, besides energy performance itself?
Energy source and energy performance improvements
What commitment must Top Management ensure the policy includes regarding compliance?
A commitment to comply with applicable legal requirements and other requirements related to its energy use, consumption and efficiency
What is the main output document of Top Management's commitment regarding energy direction?
The energy policy
Who formally expresses the energy policy (3.14)?
Top management
When considering long-term planning, what specific improvements related to energy must Top Management consider?
Energy performance improvements
What is one way Top Management can support the importance of energy management through personnel recognition?
Through rewards and participation
What specific management activity must Top Management ensure is conducted at determined intervals?
Management reviews
What must Top Management ensure regarding the results that are measured?
That results are measured and reported
What must Top Management ensure is reported regarding the measured results?
That results are reported at determined intervals
What is the necessary foundation for the entire EnMS that Top Management must define, establish, implement, and maintain?
An energy policy
What specific input to the management review must include the results of the internal audit?
EnMS audit results
What input to the management review ensures continuity from previous reviews?
Follow-up actions from previous management reviews
What must Top Management ensure regarding the EnPIs in relation to the organization?
That EnPIs are appropriate to the organization
What must Top Management ensure regarding the skills and competence of the appointed management representative?
That the management representative is appointed with appropriate skills and competence
What type of resources must Top Management ensure are available to achieve objectives and targets?
Necessary resources
What must Top Management ensure is maintained concerning the EnMS effectiveness?
Its continually improved effectiveness
What must Top Management ensure is established for the management review, besides the review itself?
That management reviews are conducted at planned intervals
What specific input related to the energy policy itself must be included in the management review?
Review of the energy policy
What specific input related to energy performance metrics must be included in the management review?
Review of energy performance and related EnPIs
What must Top Management ensure is provided when communicating the importance of energy management?
Communication to those in the organization
What must Top Management consider when making decisions or actions related to resource allocation during the management review?
The needs of the EnMS consistent with the organization's commitment to continual improvement
What must the organization ensure is provided to the management representative regarding support for energy management activities?
Top management ensures that authorized person(s) are identified to work with the management representative in support of energy management activities
Who is responsible for directing and controlling the organization at the highest level (3.28)?
Top management
What two elements must Top Management define and document for the EnMS scope?
The scope and boundaries
What must Top Management ensure the organization is continually committed to besides improving energy performance?
Continual improvement of the EnMS
Top Management must ensure the policy includes a commitment to ensure the availability of what two specific planning necessities?
Information and necessary resources to achieve objectives and targets
What specific organizational planning must Top Management consider energy performance within?
Long-term planning
What is the overall set of requirements that Top Management must ensure the EnMS conforms to?
The requirements of this International Standard
Top Management must ensure the policy is appropriate to the nature and scale of the organization’s energy use and what other energy metric?
Energy consumption
What input to the management review provides proactive recommendations for future actions?
Recommendations for improvement
What organizational process must Top Management establish, implement, and maintain?
An energy policy
What must Top Management ensure is established regarding performance goals, besides objectives?
Targets
What must Top Management ensure is provided besides technology, specialized skills, and financial resources?
Human resources
Top Management must ensure the policy includes a commitment to continual improvement in what specific area?
Energy performance
What management responsibility ensures the organization reviews its energy performance?
Conducting management reviews
What specific input to the management review requires a "review" of the foundational document?
Review of the energy policy
What specific input to the management review requires a "review" of performance metrics?
Review of energy performance and related EnPIs
What must Top Management ensure is communicated across the organization regarding energy management?
The importance of energy management
What must Top Management ensure is established for the management representative, relating to capability?
Appropriate skills and competence
Top Management must ensure the EnMS is maintained in a state of what, regarding its effectiveness?
Continual improvement of its effectiveness
What kind of input to the management review involves the evaluation of compliance?
Results of the evaluation of compliance with legal requirements and changes in legal requirements and other requirements
What is the general management process that incorporates energy management into everyday organizational practices, supported by Top Management?
The Plan - Do - Check - Act (PDCA) framework
Who is responsible for the overall intentions and direction of an organization related to its energy performance?
Top management
What must Top Management ensure is identified when conducting long-term planning, besides energy source and performance improvements?
Energy performance
What must Top Management ensure is maintained for the EnMS, besides implementation and establishment?
Maintenance
What specific output from the management review addresses potential modifications to the measurement framework?
Decisions or actions related to changes to the EnPIs
What specific output from the management review addresses potential modifications to the resource budget?
Decisions or actions related to changes to allocation of resources
What must Top Management ensure is supported by the Energy Policy, specifically regarding acquisition activities?
The purchase of energy-efficient products and services
What must Top Management ensure is supported by the Energy Policy, specifically regarding facility creation or modification?
Design for energy performance improvement
How can Top Management support the importance of energy management through employee empowerment?
Through empowerment
What commitment must Top Management ensure is included in the policy regarding legal and other requirements?
A commitment to comply with applicable legal requirements and other requirements
What is the organization fundamentally required to do with the key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance?
The organization shall ensure that the key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance are monitored, measured and analysed at planned intervals
When must the key characteristics of operations be monitored, measured, and analyzed?

What is the mandatory list of items that the key characteristics monitored must include, at a minimum?

Key characteristics shall include at a minimum: significant energy uses and other outputs of the energy review; relevant variables related to significant energy uses; EnPIs; the effectiveness of the action plans; and evaluation of actual versus expected energy consumption
What must the monitoring and measurement process assess regarding the energy management action plans?

What specific evaluation must be performed regarding energy usage during monitoring and analysis?

What must be recorded regarding the monitoring and measurement process?

What plan must be defined and implemented to govern the measurement activities?

What two factors must the energy measurement plan be appropriate to?

What must the organization define and periodically review regarding its data collection?

What two qualities must the data provided by monitoring and measurement equipment possess?
The equipment must provide data which are accurate and repeatable
What specific type of records must be maintained to establish data quality?
Records of calibration and other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability shall be maintained
What action must the organization take in response to major shifts in energy results?

What is the required administrative status of the results of the investigation and response activities?
Results of these activities shall be maintained

Significant energy uses
What outputs from the energy review, besides significant energy uses, must be monitored?
Other outputs of the energy review

What is the specific metric used for monitoring and measuring energy performance that must be included as a key characteristic?
EnPIs (Energy Performance Indicators)

What dictates the complexity of the energy measurement plan?
The size and complexity of the organization and its monitoring and measurement equipment

What specific organizational action confirms that equipment is providing repeatable data?
Maintaining records of calibration and other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability
What must the organization investigate and respond to when performance is measured?
Significant deviations in energy performance
What specific energy metric are the key characteristics of operations primarily intended to determine?
Energy performance

Checking

What must the results of the "Check" phase be used for?
To report the results

It shall be defined and implemented
The complexity of the measurement plan is related to the size and complexity of the organization and what else?

Its measurement needs
What must the monitoring process ensure is measured about the EnPIs?
The EnPIs themselves
What must be analysed at planned intervals according to this clause?
The key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance
What is the required administrative status of the results from monitoring and measurement of key characteristics?
They shall be recorded
The scope of the monitoring includes the variables related to what specific energy planning output?
Significant energy uses
What must the organization investigate when performance drops substantially?
Significant deviations in energy performance
The monitoring clause requires the organization to review its measurement needs on what basis?
Periodically
What must the organization ensure about the equipment used for key characteristic measurements?
It provides data which are accurate and repeatable
What specific aspect of the action plans is monitored?
The effectiveness of the action plans
What specific comparison must be made regarding energy consumption?
Actual versus expected energy consumption
What must the organization ensure is done to the results from monitoring and measurement?
They shall be recorded

Measurement can range from only utility meters for small organizations up to complete monitoring and measurement systems

An energy measurement plan
What does the maintenance of calibration records help establish?
Accuracy and repeatability of the monitoring equipment
What must the organization do in response to identified significant deviations?
Investigate and respond to them
What are the required inputs for the checking process to occur?
The key characteristics of its operations
What must the monitoring, measurement, and analysis of key characteristics ensure about the EnPIs?
That EnPIs are being measured
What general organizational planning concept is the energy measurement plan dependent on?
The size and complexity of the organization
What must the organization maintain records of besides calibration?
Other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability
What administrative step must follow the investigation and response to significant deviations?
Results of these activities shall be maintained
What must the organization define regarding its data collection requirements?
Its measurement needs
What minimum standard of data quality must the equipment provide?
The data must be accurate
What minimum standard of data consistency must the equipment provide?
The data must be repeatable
The monitoring and measurement process must assess the effectiveness of action plans in achieving what two planning elements?
Objectives and targets
The energy measurement plan should be appropriate to the organization's monitoring and what else?
Its measurement equipment
What must the organization maintain to provide evidence of equipment validity?
Records of calibration
If the monitoring reveals a significant deviation in energy performance, what must the organization do first?
Investigate the deviation

The key characteristics of its operations that determine energy performance
What must be included as a key characteristic that arises from the energy review?
Other outputs of the energy review
What must be recorded regarding the characteristics being monitored?
The results from monitoring and measurement of the key characteristics
How often must the monitoring, measurement, and analysis be performed?
At planned intervals

It shall be implemented
What must be periodically reviewed concerning data acquisition standards?
The organization shall periodically review its measurement needs
What must the organization ensure is accurate and repeatable?
The data provided by the equipment used in monitoring and measurement
What must the organization maintain to ensure the accuracy of equipment?
Records of calibration
What action is required after investigating a significant deviation in energy performance?
The organization must respond to the deviation
The key characteristics monitored must include the evaluation of actual versus what kind of energy consumption?
Expected energy consumption
What must the key characteristics include regarding significant energy uses?
Significant energy uses themselves
The energy measurement plan must be appropriate to the organization's complexity and its what?
Monitoring and measurement equipment
What must the organization define and implement regarding its measurement strategy?
An energy measurement plan
What must be recorded regarding the activities performed under this clause?
The results from monitoring and measurement
What determines energy performance that must be monitored and measured?
The key characteristics of its operations
What minimum quality must the equipment records support, besides repeatability?
Accuracy
What minimum quality must the equipment records support, besides accuracy?
Repeatability
What must be recorded in addition to the results of monitoring and measurement?
Results of these activities (investigating deviations) shall be maintained
What must the organization review periodically, related to data gathering capabilities?
Its measurement needs
What is the minimum requirement for the measurement scope regarding energy planning outputs?
Significant energy uses and other outputs of the energy review
What specific energy metric are the EnPIs related to?
Energy performance
What must the measurement plan include considerations for, besides the size and complexity of the organization?
Its monitoring and measurement equipment
The organization shall ensure that the key characteristics of its operations are monitored, measured, and what else?
Analysed
What is the specific required record related to proving equipment data quality?
Records of calibration
What must the organization ensure regarding the data obtained from monitoring equipment?
It provides data which are accurate and repeatable
What organizational actions are required in response to significant deviations in energy performance?
Investigate and respond
The key characteristics must include the relevant variables related to which specific energy uses?
Significant energy uses
What must be recorded as results from the checking process?
Results from monitoring and measurement of the key characteristics
What determines the complexity requirements of the energy measurement plan?
The size and complexity of the organization
What must the organization define regarding its measurement plan?
It shall be defined
What must be maintained regarding the results of investigating deviations?
Results of these activities
What is the term for large, unexpected drops in measured energy performance?
Significant deviations in energy performance
What two elements must the action plans be effective in achieving?
Objectives and targets
What administrative requirement ensures that equipment validation is tracked?
Records of calibration and other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability shall be maintained
What are the items that must be included in the key characteristics monitored, specifically related to variables?
The relevant variables related to significant energy uses
What must the organization ensure regarding its measurement equipment?
It provides data which are accurate and repeatable
What must the organization define and periodically review?
Its measurement needs
What is the organizational purpose of maintaining records of deviation investigations?
To maintain the results of these activities
What specific measurement tool is mentioned as a basic example for small organizations?
Utility meters
What must the organization ensure regarding its key characteristics?
They are monitored, measured and analysed
What must the organization ensure about the EnPIs in the monitoring process?
That EnPIs are measured
What specific organizational planning dictates the intervals for monitoring and measurement?
Planned intervals
What must the organization ensure about the implementation of the energy measurement plan?
It shall be implemented
What must be maintained as evidence of equipment validation besides calibration records?
Other means of establishing accuracy and repeatability
What specific energy data comparison is required as a key characteristic?
Evaluation of actual versus expected energy consumption
If monitoring reveals a significant deviation, what is the mandatory immediate action (two steps)?
Investigate and respond
What kind of systems can small organizations rely on for measurement, according to the guidance?
Measurement can range from only utility meters
What specific output from the energy review must be monitored?
Other outputs of the energy review
What is the general term for the information that must be recorded from the measurement activities?
Results
What must the organization define and implement, appropriate to its size and complexity?
An energy measurement plan
What two qualities of data must the records of calibration help establish?
Accuracy and repeatability
What are the required administrative actions for the results of the deviation investigation?
They shall be maintained
The monitoring and measurement process must assess the effectiveness of what plans?
The action plans
What must the organization periodically review, related to data acquisition?
Its measurement needs
What specific energy planning result must be included in the key characteristics monitored?
Significant energy uses
What must the organization ensure is done to the key characteristics at planned intervals?
Monitored, measured and analysed
What specific characteristic is required for the records of calibration?
They shall be maintained
What does the evaluation of actual versus expected energy consumption help determine?
Whether the organization is meeting its expected energy performance
The energy measurement plan must be appropriate to what two aspects of the organization?
The size and complexity of the organization
The measurement plan's complexity is also dictated by the organization's monitoring and what else?
Measurement equipment
What must be measured and reported to Top Management during the "Check" phase?
Processes and the key characteristics of operations that determine energy performance
What must the organization investigate and respond to?
Significant deviations in energy performance
What must the organization ensure regarding its measurement needs?
They shall be defined and periodically reviewed
What is the required administrative status of the results from monitoring and measurement?
They shall be recorded
At what frequency must the organization conduct internal audits of its Energy Management System (EnMS)?
Internal audits must be conducted at planned intervals
What is the primary function of the internal audit process regarding conformity?
To ensure that the EnMS conforms to planned arrangements for energy management
What foundational standard must the EnMS conform to, as determined by the internal audit?
The EnMS must conform to the requirements of this International Standard
What specific energy performance commitments must the internal audit verify conformity against?
The audit must ensure the EnMS conforms with the energy objectives and targets established
What two characteristics must the audit determine regarding the implementation of the EnMS?
The audit must ensure the EnMS is effectively implemented and maintained
What specific performance outcome must the audit ensure the EnMS achieves?
The audit must ensure the EnMS improves energy performance
What document must be developed to structure the internal audit activities?
An audit plan and schedule shall be developed
What information from prior checking activities must be considered when developing the audit plan and schedule?
The results of previous audits must be considered
What two aspects of the processes and areas to be audited must be considered when planning the audit schedule?
The status and importance of the processes and areas to be audited must be considered
What two fundamental qualities must the selection and conduct of auditors ensure regarding the audit process?
The selection and conduct of audits shall ensure objectivity and impartiality of the audit process
What administrative requirement is mandatory regarding the findings of the internal audit?
Records of the audit results shall be maintained
Who must receive the report detailing the audit results?
Audit results must be reported to top management
What must the organization ensure regarding its energy management audit arrangements, in addition to conforming to the standard's requirements?
The audit must ensure conformity to planned arrangements for energy management
What is the formal definition of an internal audit?
A systematic, independent and documented process for obtaining evidence and evaluating it objectively in order to determine the extent to which requirements are fulfilled
What is the primary action taken by auditors regarding evidence during the audit process?
The process is for obtaining evidence and evaluating it objectively
What criteria determine the scheduling of internal audits?
The scheduling is determined by planned intervals
In which phase of the Plan-Do-Check-Act framework does the internal audit activity occur?
The internal audit is part of the Checking phase
Who can perform internal audits of the EnMS on behalf of the organization?
Personnel from within the organization, or by external persons selected by the organization, working on its behalf
What must the persons conducting the audit be in a position to do regarding their judgment?
They should be in a position to do so impartially and objectively
What general requirement related to skills must the persons conducting the audit possess?
They should be competent
In smaller organizations, how can an auditor demonstrate independence?
By an auditor being free from responsibility for the activity being audited
If an organization chooses to combine its EnMS audits with other internal audits (e.g., quality), what two elements must be clearly defined for each audit?
The intent and scope of each should be clearly defined
What key distinction is made between an internal audit of the EnMS and an energy audit?
An energy audit or assessment is not the same concept as an internal audit of an EnMS
What is the primary information typically included in the outputs of an energy audit?
Information on current consumption and performance
What is the non-fulfilment of a requirement called?
A nonconformity
What organizational action is taken to eliminate a detected nonconformity?
Correction
What organizational action is taken to eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity?
Corrective action
What organizational action is taken to eliminate the cause of a potential nonconformity?
Preventive action
What must the organization review first when addressing nonconformities or potential nonconformities?
Reviewing nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What must the organization determine following the review of a nonconformity?
Determining the causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
After determining the causes, what must the organization evaluate the need for?
Evaluating the need for action to ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
What specific records must be maintained regarding actions taken to address nonconformities?
Records of corrective actions and preventive actions
What is the final step after determining and implementing corrective or preventive action?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
Corrective and preventive actions must be appropriate to the magnitude of the actual or potential problems and what other consequence?
The energy performance consequences encountered
If necessary changes are required to the system based on audit findings, who must ensure these changes are implemented?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What must the internal audit report be used as input for, conducted by Top Management?
Management Review
What must the organization determine and implement regarding actions needed to address nonconformities?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
What key input, reflecting past performance, must the management review include that is directly derived from the checking phase?
EnMS audit results
What is the required state of the audit process regarding subjectivity?
The audit process must ensure objectivity
What is the required state of the audit process regarding bias?
The audit process must ensure impartiality
What must the audit plan and schedule take into account regarding process importance?
The importance of the processes and areas to be audited
What must the audit plan and schedule take into account regarding process current state?
The status of the processes and areas to be audited
What administrative procedure ensures the audit plan is executed correctly?
The audit plan and schedule shall be developed
The audit determines conformity to what two types of established planning arrangements?
Planned arrangements for energy management and the requirements of this International Standard
What must the organization ensure is done with the audit results besides maintaining records?
They shall be reported to top management
What must the management review input include regarding follow-up actions?
Follow-up actions from previous management reviews
What record must be maintained regarding the resolution of nonconformities?
Records of corrective actions and preventive actions
The internal audit serves to check that the EnMS is not only implemented, but also what?
That the EnMS is maintained
What is the primary focus of the management review input regarding the resolution of nonconformities?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
The internal audit process is systematic, independent, and what third characteristic?
Documented
What must the persons conducting the audit possess besides impartiality, objectivity, and competence?
They must possess appropriate skills
What specific records must be maintained showing the actions taken to avoid recurrence of problems?
Records of corrective actions
What specific records must be maintained showing the actions taken to avoid the initial occurrence of problems?
Records of preventive actions
When determining the appropriate action needed for nonconformities, what must the organization be doing?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
What must the internal audit ensure regarding the EnMS's ability to create better outcomes?
That the EnMS improves energy performance
What is the final required action concerning the effectiveness of the solutions implemented after a nonconformity?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
What must the internal audit process ensure regarding the establishment and maintenance of the system?
That the EnMS is effectively implemented and maintained
What must be established for the audit process to run smoothly and predictably?
An audit plan and schedule
What must the audit plan be developed to consider regarding the processes being examined?
The status and importance of the processes and areas to be audited
What is the requirement for the auditors regarding their capability?
They should be competent
What must the conduct of audits ensure regarding the integrity of the audit process?
Objectivity and impartiality of the audit process
What must the records of audit results be subjected to?
They shall be reported to top management
What must the organization address concerning nonconformities in its checking phase?
It must address actual and potential nonconformities
What action is taken specifically to prevent recurrence of problems?
Corrective action
What action is taken specifically to prevent occurrence of problems?
Preventive action
What must the organization determine when investigating nonconformities?
The causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
The organization must ensure that any necessary changes resulting from audits and nonconformities are made to what system?
The EnMS
What must the organization define and implement to ensure records, including audit results, are managed correctly?
Controls for the identification, retrieval and retention of records
What must the internal audit verify regarding the organization's adherence to its legal framework? (Related checking clause).
Compliance with legal requirements and other requirements
What kind of evidence is obtained during the internal audit process?
Objective evidence
What must the organization consider when developing the audit schedule, based on past audit performance?
The results of previous audits
What is the purpose of evaluating audit evidence objectively?
To determine the extent to which requirements are fulfilled
What must the selection of auditors ensure about the audit process?
Objectivity
What is the purpose of taking corrective action?
To eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity
What is the purpose of taking preventive action?
To eliminate the cause of a potential nonconformity
What two aspects of the nonconformity must determine the appropriateness of the resulting corrective and preventive actions?
The magnitude of the actual or potential problems and the energy performance consequences encountered
What must the organization maintain records of related to nonconformity actions?
Corrective actions and preventive actions
The management review uses the audit results to ensure the continuing suitability, adequacy, and what else of the EnMS?
Effectiveness
If Top Management decides to allocate resources as a result of the audit findings, what output item from the Management Review does this fall under?
Changes to allocation of resources
What must be included in the internal audit check regarding the established energy planning outputs?
The energy objectives and targets established
What must the organization ensure regarding changes to the EnMS that are necessary following nonconformity review?
That any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What must the internal audit confirm about the established system components, such as operational control and documentation?
That they conform to planned arrangements
What specific energy improvement commitment must the internal audit confirm is being achieved?
The improvement of energy performance
What two elements must the selection and conduct of audits ensure regarding the audit personnel?
Objectivity and impartiality
If an organization combines audits, what must they define clearly for the combined process?
The intent and scope of each audit
What specific administrative record must be maintained regarding the audit process timeline and execution details?
The audit plan and schedule
What is the general term for the organization's overall intentions and direction related to its energy performance, which is reviewed by Top Management based partly on audit results?
The energy policy
What must the organization evaluate the need for, concerning future problems?
Evaluating the need for action to ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
What specific records must be maintained regarding the audit findings themselves?
Records of the audit results
Who is responsible for appointing the external persons who may conduct internal audits on the organization's behalf?
The organization selects them
What management input includes the status of corrective actions that often stem from audit findings?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
What must be defined and implemented for the organization's records, including audit records, to ensure they can be located?
Controls for the identification and retrieval of records
What specific input to the management review ensures that past audit findings are addressed?
Follow-up actions from previous management reviews
If the audit uncovers nonconformities, the subsequent evaluation determines the causes, which may relate to a lack of what organizational quality?
A lack of competence, training, or awareness
What is the general term for the process of obtaining evidence, related to the internal audit definition?
A documented process for obtaining evidence
What is the required characteristic of the records of the management review, which includes audit results?
Records of management review shall be maintained
What must the selection of auditors ensure about the audit personnel, besides impartiality?
Objectivity
The audit aims to ensure conformity to planned arrangements for energy management, including the requirements of what document?
This International Standard
What specific organizational requirement, if not met, is defined as a nonconformity?
Non-fulfilment of a requirement
What must the organization ensure is established when nonconformities are addressed?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
What must the organization review regarding the actions taken to address nonconformities?
The effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
What must the organization maintain to demonstrate conformity to the requirements of its EnMS?
Records
What must be tracked and reported to Top Management regarding the audit records?
Audit results
What must the organization review when addressing nonconformities, both actual and potential?
Nonconformities or potential nonconformities
The internal audit is used to report on the performance of the EnMS, which is then used by Top Management to assess the system's what?
Its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness
What specific planning output must the audit check conformity against, concerning performance achievement?

What is the organization required to address regarding problems within the Energy Management System (EnMS)?
The organization shall address actual and potential nonconformities
What three actions must the organization take when addressing actual and potential nonconformities?
The organization must make corrections, and take corrective action and preventive action
What is the specific definition of a "nonconformity"?
A nonconformity is the non-fulfilment of a requirement
What is the term for the action taken to eliminate a detected nonconformity?
Correction
What is the term for the action taken to eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity?
Corrective action
What is the primary purpose of taking corrective action?
Corrective action is taken to prevent recurrence of the nonconformity
What is the term for the action taken to eliminate the cause of a potential nonconformity?
Preventive action
What is the primary purpose of taking preventive action?
Preventive action is taken to prevent occurrence of the potential nonconformity
What is the first step the organization must take when initiating the process to handle a problem?
Reviewing nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What is the second required step after reviewing a problem?
Determining the causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What must the organization determine after identifying the causes of a nonconformity?
The organization must determine and implement the appropriate action needed

Maintaining records of corrective actions and preventive actions
What is the final required step in the process of implementing corrective or preventive action?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
What must the organization ensure is done to the overall management system if necessary changes arise from the review of nonconformities?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What specific input to the management review process relates directly to the Nonconformities clause?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
What evaluation must take place to determine if the implemented action was successful?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
Corrective action addresses problems that have been detected. What type of problems does preventive action address?
Preventive action addresses potential nonconformities
What must the organization evaluate the need for action to ensure regarding nonconformities?
Evaluating the need for action to ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
What type of action is specifically designed to prevent a nonconformity from recurring?
Corrective action
What type of action is specifically designed to prevent a nonconformity from occurring in the first place?
Preventive action
If a problem is fixed temporarily without addressing the root cause, what action was taken?
Correction
What essential administrative requirement applies to both corrective actions and preventive actions?
Maintaining records of corrective actions and preventive actions
What must be determined regarding nonconformities besides their existence?
The causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
What element of the EnMS ensures that necessary actions are implemented after they are determined?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
What must be reviewed concerning both actual and potential problems?
Reviewing nonconformities or potential nonconformities
If a nonconformity is detected, what is the first required action, according to the three-part approach?
Making corrections
Why must corrective and preventive actions be proportional to the problem?
They shall be appropriate to the magnitude of the actual or potential problems
What must the actions be appropriate to, specifically concerning the energy results?
The energy performance consequences encountered
What specific records related to CPA can be used to document traceability?
Records can be used to document traceability, preventive action and corrective action
Who is responsible for ensuring that necessary changes resulting from CPA are integrated into the system?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What must the organization assess after determining the causes of a problem?
The need for action to ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
What two types of nonconformities must the organization address?
Actual and potential nonconformities
The clause defining these actions falls under which major section of the ISO Standard (Plan, Do, Check, or Act)?
Checking
If the causes of a problem are determined to be a lack of skill, which subsequent organizational activity might be required as a corrective action?

What type of action aims to eliminate the source of a future, yet-to-be-seen failure?
Preventive action
What type of action aims to eliminate the source of a past, realized failure?
Corrective action
What must be defined and implemented for the records of CPA?
Controls for the identification, retrieval and retention of records
What must the records of corrective and preventive actions remain, in terms of readability and identity?
Records shall be and shall remain legible, identifiable and traceable to the relevant activity
Why is reviewing the effectiveness of the action taken a mandatory step?
To ensure the action taken successfully eliminates the cause and prevents recurrence or occurrence
What information gathered during the checking phase is reported to Top Management regarding CPA?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
If the nonconformity involves equipment failure, what aspect must the corrective action address?
The cause of the detected nonconformity (the equipment failure)
If the organization determines that a required document was missing, what category of failure is this?
A nonconformity (non-fulfilment of a requirement)
The requirement that CPA must be appropriate to the magnitude ensures that the actions are not excessive or insufficient relative to what?
The actual or potential problems
What must the organization maintain to demonstrate conformity to the requirements of the EnMS, including the CPA process?
Records
If an internal audit reveals an area of non-compliance, what is the initial required step under the nonconformity clause?
Reviewing nonconformities
What must the organization define and implement regarding the longevity of CPA records?
Controls for the retention of records
What must the organization define and implement regarding the location of CPA records?
Controls for the retrieval of records
What must the organization define and implement regarding the clear identification of CPA records?
Controls for the identification of records
When determining the causes of nonconformities, what system element might be investigated if the cause relates to operational control?
The operational and maintenance activities related to significant energy uses
What must the organization determine regarding the solution needed to address a nonconformity?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
Can a single nonconformity have more than one root cause?
Yes, there can be more than one cause for a nonconformity
Can a single potential nonconformity have more than one root cause?
Yes, there can be more than one cause for a potential nonconformity
What must the organization do with records of corrective actions and preventive actions once they are created?
Maintaining records of corrective actions and preventive actions
What is required after the action needed is determined and implemented?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the action taken
What must the organization address by implementing correction, corrective action, and preventive action?
Actual and potential nonconformities
The process starts with reviewing nonconformities or potential nonconformities, and ends with what?
Reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
When evaluating the need for action (Step c), what specific goal does the organization aim for regarding the problem?
To ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
If a corrective action results in changes to the EnMS, what is the organization required to ensure?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS

No, a correction only eliminates the detected nonconformity; corrective action is required to eliminate the cause
When is the status of corrective and preventive actions reviewed by Top Management?
During the Management Review
The audit results (EnMS audit results) are an input to the management review. What process often follows audit results that involves corrective actions?

What is required to be maintained for the results of corrective actions?
Records of corrective actions
What is required to be maintained for the results of preventive actions?
Records of preventive actions
The nonconformity clause ensures that actions are appropriate to the magnitude of problems and what other specific consequence?
The energy performance consequences encountered
What is the general term for the documented results achieved or evidence of activities performed, required for CPA?
Record
The cause determination (Step b) applies to both actual nonconformities and what else?
Potential nonconformities
If the effectiveness review shows the action was unsuccessful, what organizational action must be taken?
The process must cycle back to determining and implementing the appropriate action needed or potentially reviewing the causes again
The requirement for CPA falls under the general section titled what?
Checking
What is the goal of evaluating the need for action (Step c)?
To ensure that nonconformities do not occur or recur
In the context of the EnMS, what must the organization eliminate the cause of when taking preventive action?
The cause of a potential nonconformity
What are the necessary changes that the organization must ensure are made to the EnMS?
Any necessary changes resulting from the nonconformity review process
The review of nonconformities covers both detected failures and what type of failures?
Potential failures
What must the organization implement after determining the appropriate action needed?
The appropriate action needed
If an internal audit finds that an established objective was not met, this is an actual nonconformity that requires what sequence of actions?
Correction, corrective action, and preventive action
The severity of the potential energy consequences dictates the scope and resources required for what kind of action?
Corrective actions and preventive actions
If a record is used to document traceability, what specific actions might this record relate to?
Traceability related to preventive action and corrective action
What aspect of the implemented solution is reviewed in the final step of the CPA process?
The effectiveness of the corrective action or preventive action taken
What term describes the non-fulfilment of any requirement specified in the EnMS documentation or the standard itself?
Nonconformity
What must be determined regarding nonconformities and potential nonconformities?
The causes of nonconformities or potential nonconformities
The action taken to eliminate a detected nonconformity is called what?
Correction
The action taken to eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity is called what?
Corrective action
What must the organization maintain records of related to nonconformity management?
Records of corrective actions and preventive actions
What must be included as an input to the management review that reflects the organizational performance in solving problems?
The status of corrective actions and preventive actions
What are the two types of problems that corrective and preventive actions must be appropriate to the magnitude of?
The actual or potential problems
If a problem is actual, what kind of action is taken to address the root cause?
Corrective action
If a problem is potential, what kind of action is taken to address the root cause?
Preventive action
What must the organization evaluate the need for action to ensure concerning recurrence?
To ensure that nonconformities do not recur
What must the organization implement and determine regarding the appropriate solution?
Determining and implementing the appropriate action needed
What is the general management responsibility for ensuring changes are made to the EnMS as a result of CPA?
The organization shall ensure that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
If a nonconformity is detected, what three resulting actions are explicitly listed as required?
Correction, corrective action and preventive action
The required review of effectiveness (Step f) applies to what two types of actions?
Corrective action or preventive action taken
When determining causes (Step b), the organization is required to analyze the root cause of what two types of failures?

What must the organization ensure is done with records of corrective actions and preventive actions?
They shall be maintained
What is required after reviewing the effectiveness of the corrective action taken?
Ensuring that any necessary changes are made to the EnMS
What management review input covers actions taken since the previous review that aimed to eliminate problems?

What is the purpose of correction versus corrective action?
Correction eliminates the detected nonconformity, while corrective action eliminates the cause
What must the organization ensure is appropriate to the potential energy performance consequences encountered?
Corrective actions and preventive actions
What must be maintained to demonstrate evidence of preventive action?
Records
What must the organization evaluate the need for to ensure nonconformities do not occur?
Action to ensure that nonconformities do not occur
