ask_wasabi_project: What is the WASABI project?

WASABI Project Overview:

Full Name: White-label Shop for Digital Intelligent Assistance and Human-AI Collaboration in Manufacturing

Type: EU-funded research project (Horizon Europe)

Goal: Help manufacturing SMEs adopt AI-powered digital assistants for improved productivity and worker well-being

Key Technologies:
- OpenVoiceOS (OVOS) - Voice assistant platform
- Natural Language Processing (NLP)
- Human-AI collaboration frameworks
- Modular, open-source components

HumanEnerDIA Role:
- One of the pilot experiments
- Energy management use case
- Demonstrates voice + text assistants
- Validates human-centric AI design

Duration: 2024-2026 (HumanEnerDIA: 12 months)

Partners: Technology providers, DIHs, manufacturing SMEs across Europe

Website: Visit WASABI consortium website for more info
Who is participating in the WASABI experiment?

WASABI HumanEnerDIA Participants:

Primary Developer:
- A Plus Engineering (Turkey) - Energy management software company, developed Intel50001 base system

Digital Innovation Hub:
- Green eDIH (Romania) - Provides technical mentoring, hosts field trials, organizes showcases

WASABI Consortium:
- Technology partners (provide OVOS, NLP frameworks)
- Training and support services
- Dissemination partners

Field Trial Site:
- Manufacturing SME in Romania (selected by Green eDIH)
- Testing ground for real factory deployment
- Provides user feedback

Indirect Beneficiaries:
- 100+ manufacturing SMEs (target reach)
- Energy managers, factory operators
- ISO 50001 consultants

Your Role: End user, tester, feedback provider to shape future development
What is HumanEnerDIA's goal in WASABI?

HumanEnerDIA Objectives:

Primary Goal: Reduce energy management complexity through human-centric AI assistance

Specific Objectives:

1. User Effort Reduction (30% target)
- Less time understanding ISO 50001
- Faster troubleshooting
- Easier data access (voice + text)

2. Engagement Improvement
- Natural interaction (ask questions, not learn software)
- Proactive guidance (warnings, tips)
- Appreciation for good actions

3. Digital Transformation
- Lower technical barrier for SMEs
- Open-source components for adoption
- Scalable to other factories

4. Knowledge Transfer
- Text chatbot: Learn ISO 50001, energy concepts
- Voice assistant: Get real-time operational data
- Interactive tutorials

5. ISO 50001 Compliance
- Automated monitoring & measurement
- Audit-ready documentation
- Action plan tracking

Success Metric: 95%+ user satisfaction, 1000+ interactions logged
What is special about the 'human-centric' approach?

Human-Centric AI in HumanEnerDIA:

Traditional EnMS:
- Users adapt to software
- Requires training, technical skills
- Focus: Technology capabilities
- Interaction: Menus, forms, queries

Human-Centric HumanEnerDIA:
- Software adapts to users
- Natural language, minimal training
- Focus: User needs, well-being
- Interaction: Conversation, guidance

Key Principles:

1. Accessibility
- Operator to executive can use
- No SQL or programming needed
- Voice OR text (user choice)

2. Transparency
- AI explains decisions
- 'Why is this anomaly?' → Get reason
- Model performance visible

3. Guidance
- Proactive tips, not just reactive
- Suggest next actions
- Prevent problems

4. Appreciation
- Positive reinforcement
- 'Great job fixing that!'
- Encourages engagement

5. Trust
- Humans make final decisions
- AI assists, doesn't dictate
- Explainable recommendations

Result: Workers trust and use the system, leading to better energy performance
How does HumanEnerDIA use OVOS?

OVOS Integration in HumanEnerDIA:

OVOS: Open Voice Operating System - open-source voice assistant platform

What OVOS Does:
- Voice queries: 'What's Compressor-1 status?'
- Real-time data: Retrieves current energy, KPIs
- Operational commands: 'Show me today's anomalies'
- 44 API endpoints: Machine status, forecasts, consumption

Custom Skills Developed:
- Energy queries (consumption, cost)
- Machine status checks
- KPI requests
- Anomaly alerts
- Forecast queries
- Cost analysis

Interaction:
```
User: 'Hey Mycroft, what's the energy consumption today?'
OVOS: 'Total factory consumption today is 4,250 kWh, 
       which is 5% below baseline prediction. 
       Great efficiency!'
```

Why OVOS (not Alexa/Google):
- Open-source (no vendor lock-in)
- Privacy (data stays on-site)
- Customizable (factory-specific commands)
- Free (no per-query charges)

Access: Via web interface or dedicated device

Complementary: Use OVOS for quick checks, chatbot for learning
What open-source components are used?

Open-Source Technology Stack:

Digital Assistants:
- OVOS (OpenVoiceOS) - Voice assistant
- Rasa - Text chatbot NLU framework

Backend:
- FastAPI - Python web framework (Analytics, Query Service)
- PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB - Time-series database
- Redis - Caching and pub/sub
- Node-RED - Data pipeline (MQTT → DB)
- Eclipse Mosquitto - MQTT broker

ML/Analytics:
- scikit-learn - Baseline modeling
- Prophet (Facebook) - Forecasting
- statsmodels - ARIMA forecasting

Visualization:
- Grafana - Dashboards and charts
- Plotly - Interactive charts

Infrastructure:
- Docker + Docker Compose - Containerization
- Nginx - Reverse proxy
- Linux - Operating system

Benefits:
- No licensing fees
- Customizable
- Community support
- Transparent (audit source code)
- Vendor independence

GPL License: Core EnMS features to be released as open-source for SME adoption

How is HumanEnerDIA different from other energy management systems?

HumanEnerDIA Differentiators:

vs Traditional EnMS:

Traditional:
- Dashboard-only interface
- Requires training
- Reactive (you check data)
- Technical users only
- Closed-source, expensive

HumanEnerDIA:
- Voice + Text + Dashboards
- Intuitive, minimal training
- Proactive (alerts, advice)
- All skill levels
- Open-source option

Unique Features:

1. Dual Assistant:
- OVOS for operations ('What's status now?')
- Rasa for learning ('Explain ISO 50001')
- First system with both

2. Human-Centric Design:
- Conversational interaction
- Proactive guidance
- Appreciation mechanism
- Transparency (explainable AI)

3. ISO 50001 Integration:
- Built-in compliance features
- Audit documentation automated
- EnPI calculations standard

4. Open-Source Core:
- No vendor lock-in
- Customizable
- Community-driven

5. WASABI Validated:
- Research-backed approach
- Field-tested in real factory
- EU standards compliance

Target: SMEs who need powerful EnMS but lack IT resources

What is WASABI project?

WASABI Project Overview:

Full Name: White-label Shop for Digital Intelligent Assistance and Human-AI Collaboration in Manufacturing

Type: EU-funded research project (Horizon Europe)

Goal: Help manufacturing SMEs adopt AI-powered digital assistants for improved productivity and worker well-being

Key Technologies:
- OpenVoiceOS (OVOS) - Voice assistant platform
- Natural Language Processing (NLP)
- Human-AI collaboration frameworks
- Modular, open-source components

HumanEnerDIA Role:
- One of the pilot experiments
- Energy management use case
- Demonstrates voice + text assistants
- Validates human-centric AI design

Duration: 2024-2026 (HumanEnerDIA: 12 months)

Partners: Technology providers, DIHs, manufacturing SMEs across Europe

Website: Visit WASABI consortium website for more info
